program_outcomes: Bilgisayar Mühendisliği Lisans Programı mezunları; (1) ulusal/uluslararası kurumlarında tasarım, üretim, uygulama ve AR-GE çalışmalarının farklı aşamalarında başarı ile görev alabilecek, (2) ulusal/uluslararası üniversite ve araştırma kurumlarında bilim ve teknolojik gelişmeleri takip ederek lisansüstü eğitim ve araştırma yapabilecek, (3) yeni teknolojiler içeren çözümler/ürünler geliştiren şirketler kurabilecektir.

courses.name: ATATÜRK PRINCIPLES AND REVOLUTION HISTORY I
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide information about the establishment process of the Republic of Turkey and Atatürk's principles and revolutions.
courses.description: This course covers developments and changes in the 19th century world, modernization efforts in the Ottoman Empire in the first half of the 19th century, the Young Ottomans, constitutionalism and constitutional movements, modernization in the reign of Abdulhamid II, the road to the Second Constitutional Era, opposition movements, the Committee of Union and Progress, the search for salvation for the country such as Ottomanism, Turkism, Islamism and Westernism, the war period including the Tripoli, Balkan and First World Wars, Defense of Rights societies, harmful societies, the Armistice of Mudros, occupations, Mustafa Kemal Pasha and the planning of the War of Independence, Turkey's strategic issues, Turkish-Armenian relations, Turkish-Greek relations, the establishment of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey, and the Eastern and Southern fronts in the War of Independence.
learning_outcomes: Explains the basic concepts related to Atatürk's Principles and the history of the Turkish Revolution.
Explains the concepts of reform, revolution, and uprising.
Explains the enlightenment process of the Turkish nation by understanding Atatürk's basic principles and tenets.
Understands the effect of Atatürk's principles and revolutions on the establishment and development of the Republic of Turkey.
Understands Turkey's modernization process by examining the political and social changes during Atatürk's period.
Evaluates the effect of Atatürk's reforms on Turkish society and the policies of the period, and understands Atatürk's revolutions.
Examines the reflections of Atatürk's principles in today's Turkey.
Examines Atatürk's understanding of foreign policy.
Understands Atatürk's education and culture policies.
Examines the reflections of Atatürk's principles in today's Turkey and understands Turkey's modernization process.
Evaluates the effects of Atatürk's principles and revolutions in today's social and political contexts and understands Turkey's current issues.
Understands Turkey's international relations by examining Atatürk's understanding of foreign policy.
Evaluates Atatürk's foreign policy strategies and understands Turkey's struggle for independence and sovereignty.
Understands Turkey's educational and cultural development by understanding Atatürk's education and culture policies.
Evaluates the effects of Atatürk's reforms in education and culture on Turkish society and understands Turkey's transformation in education and culture.

courses.name: TÜRK DİLİ I
courses.purpose: Türk ve yabancı öğrencilerin Türk Dili'ne yönelik yazılı ve sözlü anlatım becerilerini geliştirmek.
courses.description: Bu ders; dilin tanımı ve işlevi, Türk Dili'nin tarihi dönemleri, yeryüzündeki diller, Türkçe'nin tarihi süreçte gelişen kolları, yazılı anlatım ve kompozisyon, Türk Dili'nin bugünkü durumu ve yayılma alanları, eski Türk edebiyatı ve şiir çözümlemeleri, konuşma dili ve yazı dili, Türkçe'nin ses, biçim, cümle ve anlam bilgisi, başlıca yazım kuralları, şekil bilgisi ile yeni Türk edebiyatı ve şiir çözümlemeleri konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Türk dilinin dünya dilleri arasındaki yerini öğrenerek, yeryüzündeki yayılma alanını tanır.
Dilin tanımı ve fonksiyonunu örneklerle kavrayıp bu konuda fikir yürütür.
Dilin milletlerin hayatındaki yeri ve önemini anlayabilecek ve dilin önemini vurgulayan yazıları araştırır.
Türkçe'nin dünya dilleri arasındaki yerini belirleyebilecek, yeryüzüne dağılışını gözlemler.
Türkçe'nin tarihi devrelerini tartışır.
Türk dilinin doğru yazımını gerçekleştirir.
Türkçe'yi doğru konuşup doğru yazar.
Türkçe'nin yozlaşması hakkında kendini ifade eder.
Dili doğru kullanma, zengin bir kelime hazinesine sahip olmanın yararlarını kavrar.
Türkçe'nin ses, biçim, cümle ve anlam bilgisi hakkında bilgi sahibi olur.
Türkçe'de yapılan dil yanlışlarının sebepleri hakkında görüş bildirebilecek ve “Dil yanlışları nasıl azaltılabilir?” konusunda tartışır.
Türk Dili'nin grameriyle ilgili bilgi sahibi olur.
Dil kirlenmesi veya dil bozulması hakkında sorgulama yapar.
Türkçe'nin başka dillere etkisini hakkında bilgi sahibi olur.
Yabancı dillerin Türkçe'ye etkisi hakkında fikir yürütebilir.
Dilekçe, tutanak, rapor vb. yazılı materyalleri hazırlar.

courses.name: MATEMATİK I
courses.purpose: To provide fundamental mathematical concepts, methods, and techniques and teach their applications in engineering. It also aims to build a foundation for research and studies in engineering fields.
courses.description: This course covers functions (general review), limits and continuity, derivatives, applications of derivatives, integrals, integration techniques, applications of definite integrals, transcendental functions, and generalized improper integrals.
learning_outcomes: Interprets single-variable functions, their graphs, and limits to solve problems graphically, numerically, and algebraically.
Applies the concepts of continuity and differentiability to algebraic and advanced functions.
Uses derivative rules to compute derivatives of functions and performs applications such as rates of change, minimum and maximum values, concavity, and graph sketching.
Applies the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus and integration techniques to compute definite and indefinite integrals.
Uses integration techniques to calculate the area between curves or the volume of a solid.
Compares and computes definite integrals and generalized improper integrals.

courses.name: Physics I
courses.purpose: This course is the first in a two-semester sequence of calculus-based physics courses. It is designed to meet the needs of students in engineering and applied sciences and aims to develop critical thinking and the ability to apply fundamental physics principles effectively to problems and situations.
courses.description: This course covers units, physical quantities and vectors; motion in a straight line; motion in two and three dimensions; Newton’s laws of motion; applications of Newton’s laws; work and kinetic energy; potential energy and conservation of energy; momentum, impulse and collisions; rotation of rigid bodies; dynamics of rigid bodies; gravitation; and periodic motion.
learning_outcomes: Performs basic operations related to physical quantities and vectors.
Gains knowledge about kinematics, work, energy, impulse, momentum, rotational kinematics and dynamics, and periodic motion.
Defines physical laws and uses them in problem solving; relates them to nature.
Applies mathematical knowledge in problem solving.
Can interpret, evaluate, and analyze data by examining physical concepts and ideas.

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain computer engineering and describe its main areas of work.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to the professional engineering profession and career, an introduction to engineering design, circuits, signals and systems, probability and statistics in engineering, an introduction to computer science, data science, algorithms, machine learning and artificial intelligence, software engineering, UML, and finite automata.
learning_outcomes: Defines computer engineering.
Explains the areas of computer engineering.
Gives examples of social, professional, and ethical issues.
Conveys innovation and entrepreneurship topics.
Learns the necessary steps for designing a complex system.

courses.name: Academic Communication Skills I
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to improve students' academic and engineering professional English writing, reading, and presentation skills. It also includes activities that prepare undergraduate students for their future academic or engineering careers by enabling them to use professional English in practice.
courses.description: This course covers introduction to academic communication skills, academic style and vocabulary in engineering, sentence and expression structures in academic writing, organizational structure in academic writing, paragraph structures, steps before writing, the importance of references in academic writing and avoiding plagiarism, how to write laboratory reports, persuasive writing in terms of content and style, article writing, presentation skills: basic techniques, presentation skills: presenting data in engineering, group presentation practices, and analysis of journal and conference papers in the field of engineering.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes the rules to be considered in written communication
Evaluates the importance of communication in academic life
Analyzes the structure of English paragraphs
Analyzes the structure of English articles
Recognizes the correct ways of citing in academic papers and avoiding plagiarism

courses.name: MATEMATİK HAZIRLIK
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, gerçek sayı doğrusunu, aralık gösterimini, mutlak değeri ve doğrusal, polinom ve rasyonel eşitsizliklerin çözüm yöntemlerini tanıtmak; ayrıca öğrencilere cebirsel, üstel, logaritmik, trigonometrik ve ters trigonometrik fonksiyonlar hakkında kapsamlı bir anlayış kazandırmaktır. Bunun yanı sıra, dersi başarıyla tamamlayan öğrenciler, Calculus’ ta başarılı olmak için gerekli olan cebirsel işlem becerilerini geliştireceklerdir.
courses.description: Bu ders; Cebirin Temel Kavramları, Kartezyen Koordinatlar ve Analitik Geometri, Fonksiyonlar ve Grafikleri, Polinomlar ve Rasyonel Fonksiyonlar, Üstel ve Logaritmik Fonksiyonlar, Trigonometri, Analitik Trigonometri, Karmaşık Sayılar ve İleri Matematik Problemleri konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Reel sayı uzaylarında mutlak değer, polinom ve rasyonel ifade içeren denklem ve eşitsizlikleri çözer.
Kartezyen koordinatlarda doğru, parabol, elips ve hiperbol grafiklerini yorumlar.

Parçalı tanımlı ve temel fonksiyonlar ile bunların dönüşümlerini (öteleme, yansıma ve esnetme) hem cebirsel hem de grafiksel yöntemlerle analiz eder; bu kavramları karmaşık fonksiyonel yapılar içeren gerçek dünya problemlerini modellemek ve çözmek için uygular.
Üstel ve logaritmik fonksiyonları, özelliklerini ve ters ilişkilerini derinlemesine inceleyerek değerlendirir; büyüme ve bozunma süreçlerini analiz eder.

Trigonometrik ve ters fonksiyonları radyan ve derece ölçülerini kullanarak derinlemesine inceler; trigonometrik denklemleri cebirsel ve grafiksel yöntemlerle çözmek için bunların birim çember ilişkilerini, dönüşümlerini ve özdeşliklerini yorumlar.
Karmaşık sayıların kutupsal gösterimi ile Euler formülü arasındaki ilişkiyi araştırır; Euler formülünü kullanarak trigonometrik ve hiperbolik fonksiyonlar arasındaki cebirsel geçişleri inceler.

courses.name: MATEMATİK II
courses.purpose: Temel matematik kavram, metod ve tekniklerini verip mühendislik alanındaki uygulamalarını öğretmek. Mühendislik alanındaki araştırma ve çalışmalar için alt yapı hazırlamak.
courses.description: Bu ders; dizilerde limit, alt diziler, monoton yakınsaklık teoremi, pozitif terimli seriler, alterne seriler, mutlak ve şartlı yakınsaklık, kuvvet serileri, Taylor serileri, parametrik denklemler ve kutupsal koordinatlar, vektörler ve uzay geometrisi, çok değişkenli fonksiyonlar, çok değişkenli fonksiyonların ekstremum değerleri ve çok katlı integraller konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Kutupsal koordinatlar kavramını konik kesitlerin gösterimini, eğri uzunluklarını ve eğriler ile sınırlanan bölgelerin alanlarını bulmak için kullanır.

Uzayda bir noktanın düzleme olan uzaklığını, paralel yüzlülerin hacimlerini, paralel kenarın ve üçgenin alanını, eksenlerin yönelimini, vektörler arasındaki açıların hesaplanması için iç çarpım ve vektörel çarpımı uygular.
Çok değişkenli fonksiyonların limitini, kısmı türevlerini, yönlü türevlerini, ekstrem değerlerini, teğet düzlemleri grafik, sayısal ve cebirsel olarak hesaplamak için tanır.
Alan ve hacim hesabı için çok katlı integralleri kullanır.
Dizilerin ve serilerin yakınsaklığını ve ya ıraksaklığını belirler.
Bir fonksiyonun kuvvet ve Taylor serilerini bulur.

courses.name: Introduction to Programming
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamentals of programming, problem solving, and algorithm development for students with little or no prior programming experience using the Python programming language. It aims to prepare students for more advanced programming courses and to provide an independent foundation in computational thinking for engineering and programming-related work.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to computers, programming and Python; basic programming concepts; mathematical functions, strings and objects; selection statements; loops; functions; lists, multidimensional lists, tuples, sets and dictionaries; basic and advanced GUI programming; inheritance and polymorphism; files and error handling; recursion; and efficient algorithm development.
learning_outcomes: Understand programming concepts and techniques using the Python language.
Use control statements, loops, functions, and lists.
Understand the differences between procedural and object-oriented paradigms.
Develop custom classes using encapsulation, polymorphism, inheritance, and abstraction.
Learn how to use files, exceptions, and create graphical user interfaces (GUIs).
Analyze and design strategies to solve basic programming problems.

courses.name: Tıbbi Biyoloji
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain cellular activities at the molecular level. It also aims to provide knowledge about the mechanisms underlying some diseases and to give practice on basic experiments that can be used in biology and molecular biology.
courses.description: This course covers the chemical components of the cell, proteins, DNA and chromosomes, DNA replication, repair and recombination, protein synthesis, manipulation of protein, DNA and RNA plus cell imaging, membrane structure and transport, intracellular compartments and intracellular trafficking, cell communication, cytoskeleton, mechanics of cell division, cell adhesion sites/extracellular matrix, development of multicellular organisms, histology, immune system I, and the immune system.
learning_outcomes: Defines basic biological processes such as transcription and translation and designs possible solutions to solve related problems.
Designs and performs basic biology experiments.
Analyzes current problems related to basic biological concepts using the acquired knowledge base.
Organizes and analyzes experimental results and expresses them in their own words.
Understands the basis of the formation of some diseases and produces alternative solutions.

courses.name: GENEL KİMYA
courses.purpose: The course aims to strengthen students' basic chemistry knowledge by expanding and deepening fundamental concepts. It covers core topics such as matter, atoms, chemical compounds and reactions, and continues with the ideal gas law, atomic spectra, and quantum theory, followed by periodic trends, chemical bonding theories, intermolecular interactions, physical properties of solutions, chemical equilibrium, and acids and bases.
courses.description: This course covers matter, atoms and atomic theories, chemical compounds, chemical reactions, aqueous solution reactions, gases, thermochemistry, electronic structure of the atom, periodic table and atomic properties, chemical bonds I-basic concepts, chemical bonds II-bond theories, intermolecular forces, solutions and their physical properties, chemical equilibrium, and acids and bases.
learning_outcomes: Remembers the basic concepts of chemistry in their current forms.
Predicts chemical compounds, their reactions, and the role of a compound in a reaction.
Evaluates gas laws.
Recognizes atom and intermolecular interactions.
Evaluates solution properties and components.
Predicts the relationship between temperature, heat, and work in chemical reactions.
Evaluates bonding theories.
Recognizes acid-base reactions.
Evaluates chemical equilibrium conditions.

courses.name: ATATÜRK İLKELERİ VE İNKILAP TARİHİ II
courses.purpose: Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin kuruluş süreci ve Atatürk İlke ve İnkılâpları hakkında bilgiler vermek.
courses.description: Bu ders; Kurtuluş Savaşı’nda Batı Cephesi, Zaferin Kazanılması ve Mudanya Mütarekesi, Saltanatın Kaldırılması ve İstanbul ile ilişkiler, Lozan Barış Antlaşması, Cumhuriyet’in ilanı, Halifeliğin kaldırılışı, 1924 Anayasası, Atatürk İnkılâplarının hayata geçişi, toplumsal, hukuk, eğitim ve kültür alanlarında yapılan inkılâplar, çok partili hayata geçiş denemeleri, 1929 ekonomik buhranı ve Cumhuriyetin kalkınma politikaları, Atatürk’ün dış politikası, Atatürk sonrası Türkiye, II. Dünya Savaşı ve çok partili hayata geçiş, Türkiye’nin stratejik konuları, Musul sorunu, Hatay’ın anavatana katılması, Boğazlar meselesi ve Kıbrıs konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Atatürk İlkelerini ve önemini açıklar.
Avrupa ve dünyadaki gelişmelerin Türkiye Cumhuriyetine etkilerini açıklar.
Avrupa ve dünyadaki politikaların Türkiye'ye etkilerini ve sonuçlarını açıklar.
Kapitalizm/emperyalizmin Türkiye'ye etkilerini tanımlar.
Komşularıyla Türkiye arasındaki ilişkisini/sorunları açıklar.
Türkiye'nin Avrupa ve Dünyadaki yerini /önemini açıklayabilecektir.

courses.name: TÜRK DİLİ II
courses.purpose: Türk ve yabancı öğrencilerin Türk Dili'ne yönelik yazılı ve sözlü anlatım becerilerini geliştirmek.
courses.description: Bu ders; öz geçmiş, kelime türleri, birleşik kelimelerin yazılışı, fiil çatıları, vurgu, kelime grupları, Türk tasavvuf edebiyatı ve şiir çözümlemeleri, paragraf, sözlü anlatım türleri, anlatım bozuklukları, cümle bilgisi, cümle çeşitleri, halk edebiyatı ve şiir çözümlemeleri konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Kelimeler ve kelimelerin cümledeki yerini karşılaştırır.
Kelime türleri ve gruplarını analiz eder.
Cümlenin ögelerini ayırır.
Anlamları bakımından cümleleri ayırt eder.
Anlatım özelliklerini ifade eder.
Yazım kurallarını tanımlar.
Anlatım biçimlerini ifade eder.
Anlatım türlerini ayırt eder.
Yazılı eserlerin çözümlemelerini analiz eder.
Türkçe sözlükleri kullanır.
Şiir çözümlerini yapar.
Hikaye çözümleri yapar.

courses.name: MATEMATİK I
courses.purpose: To provide fundamental mathematical concepts, methods, and techniques and teach their applications in engineering. To build a foundation for research and studies in engineering.
courses.description: This course covers functions (general review), limits and continuity, derivatives, applications of derivatives, integrals, integration techniques, applications of definite integrals, transcendental functions, and generalized integrals.
learning_outcomes: Interpret single-variable functions, their graphs, and limits to solve them graphically, numerically, and algebraically.
Apply the concepts of continuity and differentiability to algebraic and advanced functions.
Calculate derivatives of functions using derivative rules and perform applications such as rates of change, minimum and maximum values, concavity, and graph sketching.
Apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus and integration techniques to compute definite and indefinite integrals.
Use integration techniques to calculate the area between curves or the volume of a solid.
Compare and compute definite integrals and generalized (improper) integrals.

courses.name: MATEMATİK HAZIRLIK
courses.purpose: This course introduces the real number line, interval notation, absolute value, and solution methods for linear, polynomial, and rational inequalities. It also aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of algebraic, exponential, logarithmic, trigonometric, and inverse trigonometric functions, and to develop the algebraic manipulation skills needed for success in Calculus.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts of algebra, Cartesian coordinates and analytic geometry, functions and their graphs, polynomial and rational functions, exponential and logarithmic functions, trigonometry, analytic trigonometry, complex numbers, and advanced mathematical problems.
learning_outcomes: Solves equations and inequalities involving absolute value, polynomial, and rational expressions in real number spaces.
Interprets graphs of lines, parabolas, ellipses, and hyperbolas in Cartesian coordinates.

Analyzes piecewise-defined and basic functions and their transformations (translation, reflection, and stretching) using both algebraic and graphical methods; applies these concepts to model and solve real-world problems involving complex functional structures.
Evaluates exponential and logarithmic functions, their properties, and inverse relationships in depth; analyzes growth and decay processes.

Examines trigonometric and inverse functions in depth using radian and degree measures; interprets unit circle relationships, transformations, and identities to solve trigonometric equations algebraically and graphically.
Investigates the relationship between the polar form of complex numbers and Euler's formula; uses Euler's formula to examine algebraic transitions between trigonometric and hyperbolic functions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Cartesian Coordinates and Analytic Geometry

courses.name: MATEMATİK II
courses.purpose: To provide fundamental mathematical concepts, methods, and techniques and teach their applications in engineering fields. It also aims to prepare a foundation for research and studies in engineering.
courses.description: This course covers sequences and series, including limits of sequences, subsequences, monotone convergence theorem, positive term series, alternating series, absolute and conditional convergence, and power series with interval and radius of convergence, term-by-term integration and differentiation, and Taylor series. It also includes parametric equations and polar coordinates, vectors and solid geometry, multivariable functions such as limits, continuity, partial and directional derivatives, extrema and Lagrange multipliers, and multiple integrals in Cartesian, cylindrical, and spherical coordinates.
learning_outcomes: Uses polar coordinates to represent conic sections and to find arc lengths and areas of regions bounded by curves.

Applies dot product and cross product to compute the distance from a point to a plane, volumes of parallelepipeds, areas of parallelograms and triangles, axis orientations, and angles between vectors.
Recognizes limits, partial derivatives, directional derivatives, extrema, and tangent planes of multivariable functions graphically, numerically, and algebraically.
Uses multiple integrals to calculate area and volume.
Determines whether sequences and series converge or diverge.
Finds power series and Taylor series of a function.

courses.name: FİZİK II
courses.purpose: Öğrencilerin temel elektrik ve manyetizma konularında teorik bilgi edinmesini ve bu teorik yapıyla evreni ve mühendislik uygulamalarını en etkili şekilde anlayabilmelerini sağlamaktır.
courses.description: Bu ders; elektrik yükü ve elektrik alanı, Gauss yasası, elektrik potansiyeli, kapasitans ve dielektrikler, akım, direnç ve elektromotor kuvvet, doğru akım devreleri, manyetik alan ve manyetik kuvvetler, manyetik alan kaynakları, elektromanyetik indükleme, indüktans I, indüktans II, alternatif akım, elektromanyetik dalgalar I ve elektromanyetik dalgalar II konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Temel elektrik ve manyetizma yasalarını bilir ve problem çözümünde kullanır.
Elektrostatik, kapasitans ve dielektrik, doğru akım devreleri ve elemanları, elektromotif kuvvet konuları hakkında bilgi sahibi olur.
Temel manyetizma, elektromanyetik indükleme, indüktans, alternatif akım ve elektromanyetik dalgalar hakkında bilgi sahibi olur.
Matematik bilgilerini problem çözümünde uygulayabilme yeteneğini kazanır.
Elektrik ve manyetizma konularında temel kuramsal bilgileri kullanarak problemleri modelleyebilir, verileri yorumlayabilir, değerlendirebilir ve analiz edebilir.

courses.name: PHYSICS II LAB
courses.purpose: To support and deepen theoretical knowledge through electricity and magnetism experiments.
courses.description: This course covers electrostatics, Ohm's Law and basic measurement, Kirchhoff's Law, RC circuits, magnetic force on a current-carrying wire, transformers, and RLC circuits.
learning_outcomes: Use measuring instruments in the laboratory properly and appropriately.
Set up and operate simple electrical circuits.
Test fundamental laws of electricity and magnetism experimentally.
Compare and interpret experimental and theoretical results.
Discuss experimental results.

courses.name: ACADEMIC COMMUNICATION SKILLS II
courses.purpose: This course aims to improve students' verbal and non-verbal communication, effective listening, and note-taking skills, and to encourage them to use these skills effectively in interpersonal, group, and public contexts as well as in academic and professional settings.
courses.description: This course covers public speaking, presentation development, presentation strategies, poster preparation, in-class presentation practices, and preparing presentations using PowerPoint.
learning_outcomes: Acquires important strategies to overcome fear of public speaking
Improves public speaking skills
Prepares presentation content by researching a topic
Develops presentation skills by using strategies
Prepares effective PPT presentations and posters

courses.name: İleri Programlama
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, programlama uygulamaları, verimlilik ve veri bilimi üzerinde durularak Python kullanan öğrencilerin programlama ve problem çözme yeteneklerini ve becerilerini geliştirmektir. Öğrenciler, yapay zeka ve veri bilimi için geliştirilmiş yerleşik kitaplıklardan yararlanan verimli programları tasarlamayı, geliştirmeyi ve test etmeyi öğrenir.
courses.description: Verimli algoritma geliştirme, arama ve sıralama algoritmalarının analizi, Python veri yapıları, veri analizi ve görselleştirme, NumPy ve SciPy ile dizi tabanlı ve bilimsel programlama, Pandas ile veri işleme, veri yükleme ve depolama, dosya formatları, zaman serileri ve basit lineer regresyon, doğal dil işleme ve web veri çıkarma, Twitter veri madenciliği ve duygu analizi, JSON ve web servisleri, makine öğrenmesi, derin öğrenme, işbirlikçi filtreleme ve optimizasyon konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Verimli program tasarlama, uygulama ve test etme.
Farklı problemler için program kodunu öğrenerek, analiz ederek, çözerek ve geliştirerek programlama becerilerini geliştirme.
Yapılandırılmış programlama, soyut veri türleri, sınıflar ve nesneler kullanarak modüler programların nasıl tasarlanacağını, geliştirileceğini ve uygulanacağını öğrenme.
Birçok alanda bulunan yerleşik ve üçüncü taraf yazılım kütüphanelerinin özelliklerinden yararlanma.
Verilerin nasıl depolanacağını, yükleneceğini, değiştirileceğini ve keşfedileceğini öğrenme.
Verileri özetleme, görselleştirme ve analiz etme.
Matematik, bilim, mühendislik, finans, yapay zeka ve oyun konularında çok çeşitli problemler için program yazma.
Birkaç örnek üzerinde bazı makine öğrenimi, veri madenciliği ve optimizasyon kitaplıklarının nasıl kullanılacağını ve uygulanacağını öğrenme.

courses.name: PROGRAMLAMAYA GİRİŞ
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamentals of programming, problem solving, and algorithm development for students with little or no prior programming experience using the Python programming language. It aims to prepare students for more advanced programming courses and to provide an understanding of computational thinking in engineering as a standalone course for students who want to write programs for their studies and professional work.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to computers, programming and Python; basic programming; selection statements; data types; arrays; iteration; functions; GUIs (graphical user interfaces); objects and classes; inheritance and polymorphism; lists and multidimensional lists; sets and dictionaries; files; exceptions; and recursion. It also includes problem-oriented structured programming, algorithmic thinking, and object-based thinking.
learning_outcomes: Understand programming concepts and techniques using the Python language.
Use control statements, loops, functions, and lists.
Understand the differences between procedural and object-oriented paradigms.
Develop custom classes using encapsulation, polymorphism, inheritance, and abstraction.
Learn how to use files, exceptions, and create GUIs (Graphical User Interfaces).
Analyze and design strategies to solve basic programming problems.

courses.name: GENERAL CHEMISTRY
courses.purpose: The course aims to strengthen students' basic chemistry knowledge by expanding and deepening fundamental concepts. It introduces core topics such as matter, atoms, chemical compounds, and reactions, and continues with the ideal gas law, atomic spectra, quantum theory, periodic properties, chemical bonding theories, intermolecular interactions, physical properties of solutions, chemical equilibrium, and acids and bases.
courses.description: This course covers matter, atoms and atomic theories, chemical compounds, chemical reactions, aqueous solution reactions, gases, thermochemistry, electronic structure of the atom, periodic table and atomic properties, chemical bonding I-basic concepts, chemical bonding II-bond theories, intermolecular forces, solutions and their physical properties, chemical equilibrium, and acids and bases.
learning_outcomes: Recalls the fundamental concepts of chemistry in their current form
Predicts chemical compounds, their reactions, and the role of a compound in a reaction
Evaluates gas laws
Recognizes interactions between atoms and molecules
Evaluates solution properties and components
Predicts the relationship between temperature, heat, and work in chemical reactions
Evaluates bonding theories
Recognizes acid-base reactions
Evaluates chemical equilibrium conditions

courses.name: Introduction to Materials Science
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' analytical thinking by helping them understand the relationship between structure, properties, and processes. It also provides fundamental knowledge about modern engineering materials and examines their application areas.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to materials science and engineering, atomic structures and interatomic bonds, fundamentals of crystallography and structures of crystalline solids, structural defects in solid materials, diffusion, mechanical properties of metals, dislocations and deformation, strengthening mechanisms, fracture, fatigue and creep mechanisms, phase diagrams, phase transformation mechanisms, properties and application areas of metals, ceramics, polymers and composite materials, corrosion mechanisms and protection methods in materials, and the economic, social and environmental impacts of materials science and engineering, along with student presentations.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes basic concepts, technologies and terms related to materials science.
Evaluates elastic and plastic deformation, strengthening and mechanical properties.
Compares the structure-property-process relationship in materials.
Classifies engineering materials and identifies the effects of atomic bonding, atomic arrangement, crystal structures and bonding properties on materials.
Identifies the application areas of materials.

courses.name: Digital Logic Design
courses.purpose: By the end of the semester, students will be able to conduct experiments to learn the logic design and prototyping process, write effective technical reports for laboratory experiments, design digital circuits that include combinational and sequential logic components to solve a problem, prototype a digital logic circuit and demonstrate that it meets performance requirements, design experiments to experimentally verify a hypothesis, Boolean law or identity, and dependencies between variables, and use the latest combinational and sequential logic design methods, techniques, and paradigms.
courses.description: This course covers course overview, number systems, addition/subtraction with signed numbers, logic gates, Boolean arithmetic, synthesis, Karnaugh maps, review of covered topics, addition, subtraction, multiplication, combinational logic circuits, sequential logic circuits, registers and counters, memory and programmable logic circuits, manufacturing technology, and hardware description languages.
learning_outcomes: They can design a digital logic circuit, test it with simulation software and measurement instruments in the laboratory, and evaluate the results in written and oral form.
They can design synchronous sequential circuits using sequential logic elements such as registers and flip-flops.
They recognize combinational logic circuits (adders/subtractors, code converters, comparators, multiplexers, encoders and decoders) and use them to design larger and more complex circuits.
They can perform circuit analysis and design using combinational logic design techniques such as Karnaugh maps and truth tables.
They can formulate and solve functions using Boolean arithmetic.
They understand and use number systems, number bases and conversions, and binary codes.

courses.name: Diferansiyel Denklemler
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce differential equations, provide solution techniques, and teach their applications in engineering. It also prepares a foundation for research and studies in engineering fields.
courses.description: This course covers preliminaries on differential equations, definitions and terminology, initial value problems, methods for solving first-order differential equations such as separable, linear, exact and non-exact equations, substitution methods, and differential equations as mathematical models. It also includes higher-order linear differential equations, reduction of order, homogeneous linear equations with constant coefficients, undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters, Cauchy-Euler equations, Laplace transforms, inverse transforms, transforms of derivatives, and solving initial value problems using Laplace transforms.
learning_outcomes: Classify differential equations, recognize solutions of differential equations, differential equation systems, and initial value problems, and apply the Existence and Uniqueness Theorem for first-order differential equations.
Apply methods for solving first-order differential equations.

Recognize and solve differential equations as mathematical models and higher-order linear differential equations, and apply the Existence and Uniqueness Theorem for higher-order linear differential equations.
Recognize linearly dependent and independent solutions and the Wronskian, and apply methods for solving higher-order linear differential equations.
Solve Cauchy-Euler differential equations and initial value problems using Laplace transforms.

courses.name: MÜHENDİSLER İÇİN PROGRAMLAMA
courses.purpose: This course provides the fundamentals of the C programming language and then covers topics that enable the transition to C++. Its main aims are to help students understand algorithms and the process from the first steps of programming to testing for a given problem, to emphasize the importance of modular programming and functions, to explain pointers and dynamic memory allocation and their role in arrays and strings, and to highlight the importance of the object-oriented approach in programming.
courses.description: Introduction to computer architecture and C, development of structured programs and program control, modular programming and functions, arrays, pointer concepts, characters and strings, formatted input/output, structures, unions, bit manipulation and numbering systems, file operations, data structures, computational thinking for computer science, preprocessor directives and other related topics.
learning_outcomes: Learning the basic syntax rules of C programming.
Understanding pointers in C programming, dynamic memory allocation, arrays and strings.
Understanding the concept of functions in C and different function parameters.
Understanding the framework in which variables are defined in programming and the use of pointers in this context.
Understanding the transition to the C++ programming language.

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: To teach methods for solving systems of linear equations and to develop the ability to use matrices and determinants in practice. To help students learn concepts such as vector spaces, bases, and linear dependence, and to interpret data spaces. To enable the use of mathematical knowledge in solving basic science and engineering problems.
courses.description: This course covers preliminary topics in linear algebra, including matrices and systems of linear equations: definitions, notation, matrix algebra and terminology for linear equation systems, elementary row operations, row echelon and reduced row echelon matrices, and solutions of linear algebraic systems. It also includes Gaussian elimination, Gauss-Jordan elimination, inverse of a square matrix, determinants and the adjoint method, elementary matrices, LU factorization and Cramer's rule. Further topics are vector spaces, subspaces and spanning sets, linear dependence and independence, bases and dimension, row and column spaces, rank and nullity theorem, inner product spaces and orthogonality, eigenvalue/eigenvector problems, applications of eigenvalues and eigenvectors, diagonalization, singular value decomposition, finding the pseudoinverse, and linear transformations including kernel, image, and other properties.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes arithmetic operations on matrices, properties of matrices, elementary row operations, and finds the row echelon and reduced row echelon forms of a matrix.
Computes solutions of linear equation systems using Gaussian and Gauss-Jordan elimination methods, matrix inverse, and Gauss-Jordan method, and finds the determinant of a matrix.
Analyzes finding the inverse of a matrix by the adjoint method, elementary matrices, LU factorization, and Cramer's rule.

Recognizes the importance of concepts such as subspaces of a vector space, spanning sets, linear dependence and independence, basis and dimension, row and column spaces, rank and nullity theorem, inner product spaces, and orthogonality.

Analyzes the eigenvalues, corresponding eigenvectors and eigenspaces of a matrix, diagonalization, singular value decomposition, pseudoinverse, and linear transformations, and applies them to engineering problems.

courses.name: Object Oriented Programming
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach object-oriented programming (OOP) in a problem-oriented way, focusing more on problem solving than syntax. Students learn object-based thinking and design, improve their algorithmic thinking skills, and learn how and when to apply OOP techniques effectively to specific problems.
courses.description: This course covers a review of basic techniques in Java and then introduces objects and classes, object-oriented thinking, inheritance and polymorphism, exception handling and text I/O, abstract classes and interfaces, Java GUI basics, event-driven programming and animations, Java UI controls and multimedia, binary I/O, recursion, generics, lists, stacks, queues and priority queues, sets and maps, and the implementation of lists, stacks, queues, and priority queues.
learning_outcomes: Define and discuss the fundamentals of object-oriented programming and its languages.
Use and apply the basic concepts and structures of object-oriented programming such as class, object, polymorphism, inheritance, and overriding.
Use basic data types, basic control structures, loop types, functions, and libraries in Java.
Use and apply classes, object-oriented thinking, inheritance and polymorphism, exception handling, abstract classes, and interfaces in Java.

Design and develop object-oriented programs using classes, inheritance, Java GUIs, event-driven programming and animations, Java UI controls and multimedia, binary I/O, recursion, generics, lists, stacks, queues and priority queues, sets and maps.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to object-oriented programming language, computer programs, introduction to the programming environment and tools, algorithm design
Hafta 2: Basic data types, variables, arithmetic operators, input and output, String types, control structures, boolean variables and operators, mixing numbers and Strings, nested branches
Hafta 3: Loops (for, do, while), nested loops, random numbers, methods, parameter passing, return values, void methods, variable scope, repeated methods
Hafta 4: Arrays and array lists, array algorithms, two-dimensional arrays
Hafta 5: Input/Output and error handling, file operations, command line operations, exception handling (try-catch-finally)
Hafta 6: Objects and classes, instance methods, constructors, object references, static variables and methods
Hafta 7: Inheritance, subclasses and methods, polymorphism, interface types
Hafta 8: Graphical user interface design libraries and advanced topics in graphical user interface design
Hafta 10: Recursion, sorting and searching (selection/merge/binary search)
Hafta 11: Java Collections Framework: Lists, Stacks, Queues and Priority Queues, Sets and Maps
Hafta 12: Implementation of Lists, Stacks, Queues and Priority Queues
Hafta 13: Web Development with Java and Introduction to Java Enterprise (J2EE)

courses.name: Probability and Random Variables
courses.purpose: This course is an introductory course on probability and random variables for engineering undergraduate students. It first presents the basic differences between statistics and probability, then focuses on probability concepts, including probability axioms, probability density functions, joint density functions, random variables, and related topics.
courses.description: This course covers course information, introduction to probability and statistics, basic probability, conditional probability, discrete random variables, discrete distributions and their statistics, continuous random variables and their statistics, continuation of continuous random variables, review of topics for the exam week, continuous distributions, multiple discrete random variables, multiple continuous random variables, conditional discrete probability distribution function, conditional continuous probability distribution function, and conditional continuous probability distribution function.
learning_outcomes: Models simple probability events mathematically.
Determines the probabilities of events in a given sample space and calculates expected value, standard deviation, and conditional probability using random variable modeling.
Develops mathematical models for discrete and continuous random variables.
Determines which situations the most commonly used probability distributions are suitable for.

Models a new random variable depending on multiple random variables and obtains joint probability density functions, conditional probability functions, and transformations of random variables in one and two dimensions.

courses.name: Discrete Mathematics
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with logical and mathematical thinking skills. It also aims to develop skills in mathematical reasoning, combinatorial analysis, discrete structures, algorithmic thinking, and application/modeling.
courses.description: This course covers basic concepts, foundations of logic, logical and conditional expressions, logic of quantified expressions, introduction to number theory, direct proof and counterexample, sequences, mathematical induction, strong induction, recursion and structural induction, introduction to set theory, functions, cardinality applications for computability, mathematical relations, equivalence relations and modular arithmetic, basic cryptography problems, basic graph theory and trees, and graph theory applications.
learning_outcomes: Determines whether an argument is valid by using logical notation.
Carries out proof writing and proof evaluation.
Understands set fundamentals, set operations, and verification of basic set equalities.
Understands the properties of functions, their relationships, and introductory knowledge of graph theory and cryptology.

courses.name: Data Structures
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach how data should be organized in a computer for effective algorithm design in solving different types of problems. It covers the use of data structures such as arrays, lists, stacks, queues, trees, heaps, graphs, and their applications in searching, sorting, selection, and similar operations. The course also introduces these topics using the Python programming language.
courses.description: Introduction to data structures and algorithms; basic Python programming and installation of AI IDE extensions; use of AI to explain complexity classes; functions, pointers, and node classes; strings, structures, and memory allocation in Python; algorithm analysis and complexity representations; fundamental data structures including linked lists, stacks, and queues; recursion and searching; sorting algorithms such as quicksort and mergesort; trees and binary search trees; priority queues and heaps; graph algorithms (BFS/DFS); hash functions and collision analysis; code refactoring and optimization; and debugging AI-generated code.
learning_outcomes: Explains the main principles of algorithm analysis.
Uses basic data structures such as arrays, lists, stacks, and queues in algorithm design.
Applies tree, binary tree, heap, hash table, and graph data structures in problem solving.
Selects the appropriate data structure for an effective solution to a problem.
Analyzes the correctness, complexity, and efficiency of an algorithmic solution.
Uses AI tools to create code templates, explain complex algorithms, and debug code efficiently.
Evaluates the correctness, efficiency, and security vulnerabilities of AI-generated code.

courses.name: BİLGİSAYAR ORGANİZASYONU
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı bilgisayar sistemleri donanımının organizasyonunu ve mimarisini, von Neumann makinesinin temellerini, komut seti mimarilerini, adresleme modlarını, montaj programlamayı, işlemci ve kontrol tasarımını, CISC ve RISC mimarilerini, bilgisayar aritmetiğini, bellek sistemlerini, ara bağlantı yapıları, G/Ç organizasyonu, ikincil depolama aygıtları, performans sorunları, paralel işleme ve çok çekirdekli bilgisayarlar ve grafik işleme birimleri. Bu dersin laboratuvarları, bilgisayar bileşenlerinin mantıksal tasarımına, montaj programlamasına ve basit bir mikroişlemci ve bileşenlerinin bir donanım tanımlama dili kullanılarak tasarımına odaklanacaktır.
courses.description: Bu ders; Bilgisayar Organziasyonu ve mimarisine giriş, bilgisayar tasarımının evrimi, bilgisayarın başlıca parçaları, bu parçaların kendi aralarında ve bilgisayar dışı parçalarla iletişimi, iç ve dış hafıza birimleri, giriş ve çıkış birimlerinin incelenmesi, bilgisayar aritmetiği, sayı düzenleri, dijital mantık, işlemcilerin iç yapısı ve organizasyonu, boruhattı, RISC mimarisi, çok işlemcili sistemler, paralel işlemciler, proje sunumları ve genel amaçlı grafik işleme birimi konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Bilgisayar organizasynou alanındaki en son mimari ve teknolojk gelişmeleri tanımak ve takip etmek
Bilgisayar donanımını tasarlamak ve gerçeklemek için gerekli modern araç ve teknikleri bulma, seçme ve kullanma becerisi
Bu alandaki teorik ve uygulamalı bilgiyi kullanarak, temel bilgisayar donanımını gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında tasarlayabilme becerisi
Bilgisayar organizasyonu konusunda yeterli bilgi birikimi kazanmak

courses.name: Mühendisler İçin Programlama
courses.purpose: Bu derste C programlama dilinin temelleri verilecektir ve daha sonra ise C++ programlama diline geçişi sağlayan konular işlenecektir. Dersin temel amaçları şunlardır: Algoritma konusunun anlaşılması ve verilen bir problem için programlamanın ilk adımlarından test aşamasına kadar olan süreçleri aktarmak, modüler programlamanın önemini kavratmak ve fonksiyonların önemini ortaya koymak, pointer ve dinamik bellek atanmasının anlaşılması ve bunlarla dizinlerin ve metin dizilerinin programlamadaki öneminin ortaya konması, ve nesne tabanlı yaklaşımın programlamadaki öneminin ortaya konması.
courses.description: Bu ders; Bilgisayar Yapısına ve C'ye Giriş, Yapısal Programların Geliştirilmesi ve Program Kontrolü, Modüler programlama ve fonksiyonlar, Array'ler : 1. Kısım, Array'ler : 2. Kısım, Pointer Kavramı : 1. Kısım, Pointer Kavramı : 2. Kısım, Ara sınav, Karakterler ve karakterler dizini (dizgeler), Formatlandırılmış Girdiler/Çıktılar, Yapılar, Birleşimler, Bit Manipülasyonları ve sayılandırma, Dosya İşlemleri, Veri yapıları, Bilgisayar Bilimi için düşünme, Önişlemci terimleri ve diğer bazı konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: C programlama için temel programlama dizim kurallarının öğrenilmesi.
C Programlamada işaretçilerin anlaşılması, dinamik bellek atanması, dizinler ve metin dizinlerinin anlaşılması.
C'de fonksiyon kavramı ve farklı fonksiyon parametrelerinin anlaşılması.
Programlamada değişkenlerin tanımlandığı çerçeve ve işaretçilerin bu husustaki kullanımı.
C++ programlama diline geçişin anlaşılması.

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: To teach solution methods for systems of linear equations and to provide the ability to use matrix and determinant concepts in practice. It also aims to help students understand vector spaces, bases, and linear dependence so they can interpret data spaces, and to use mathematical knowledge to solve basic science and engineering problems.
courses.description: This course covers matrices and systems of linear algebraic equations, including definitions, notation, matrix algebra, terminology for linear equation systems, elementary row operations, row echelon and reduced row echelon matrices, and solutions of linear algebraic systems. It also includes Gaussian elimination, Gauss-Jordan elimination, inverse of a square matrix, determinants, adjoint method, elementary matrices, LU factorization, Cramer's rule, vector spaces, subspaces, spanning sets, linear dependence and independence, bases and dimension, row and column spaces, rank and nullity theorem, inner product spaces and orthogonality, eigenvalues, eigenvectors and eigenspaces, applications of eigenvalue/eigenvector decomposition, diagonalization, singular value decomposition, pseudoinverse, and linear transformations including kernel, image, and other properties.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes arithmetic operations on matrices, properties of matrices, elementary row operations, and finds the row echelon and reduced row echelon forms of a matrix.
Computes solutions of linear equation systems using Gaussian and Gauss-Jordan elimination methods, matrix inverse, and Gauss-Jordan method, and finds the determinant of a matrix.
Analyzes finding the inverse of a matrix by the adjoint method, elementary matrices, LU factorization, and Cramer's rule.

Recognizes the importance of concepts such as subspaces of a vector space, spanning sets, linear dependence and independence, basis and dimension, row and column spaces, rank and nullity theorem, inner product spaces, and orthogonality.

Analyzes the eigenvalues, corresponding eigenvectors and eigenspaces of a matrix, diagonalization, singular value decomposition, pseudoinverse, and linear transformations, and applies them to engineering problems.

courses.name: Object-Oriented Programming
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach object-oriented programming (OOP) in a problem-oriented way, focusing more on problem solving than syntax. Students learn object-based thinking and design, improve their algorithmic thinking skills, and learn how and when to apply OOP techniques effectively to specific problems.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to object-oriented programming, basic data types, variables, arithmetic operators, input/output, String types, control structures, loops, methods, arrays and array lists, I/O and error handling, files and command-line operations, exception handling, objects and classes, instance methods, constructors, object references, static variables and methods, inheritance, subclasses and methods, polymorphism, interface types, GUI design libraries and advanced GUI topics, event-driven programming and animations, recursion, sorting and searching, Java Collections Framework, implementation of lists, stacks, queues and priority queues, sets and maps, and an introduction to Java web development and J2EE.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to define and discuss the fundamentals of object-oriented programming and languages.
Can use and apply the basic concepts and structures of object-oriented programming such as class, object, polymorphism, inheritance, and overriding.
Can use basic data types, control structures, loop types, functions, and libraries in Java.
Can use and apply classes, object-oriented thinking, inheritance and polymorphism, exception handling, abstract classes, and interfaces in Java.

Can design and develop object-oriented programs using classes, inheritance, Java GUIs, event-driven programming and animations, Java UI controls and multimedia, binary I/O, recursion, generics, lists, stacks, queues and priority queues, sets and maps.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to object-oriented programming language, computer programs, introduction to the programming environment and tools, algorithm design
Hafta 2: Basic data types, variables, arithmetic operators, input and output, String types, control structures, boolean variables and operators, mixing numbers and Strings, nested branches
Hafta 3: Loops (for, do, while), nested loops, random numbers, methods, parameter passing, return values, void methods, variable scope, repeated methods
Hafta 4: Arrays and array lists, array algorithms, two-dimensional arrays
Hafta 5: Input/output and error handling, file operations, command-line operations, exception handling (try-catch-finally)
Hafta 6: Objects and classes, instance methods, constructors, object references, static variables and methods
Hafta 7: Inheritance, subclasses and methods, polymorphism, interface types
Hafta 8: Graphical user interface design libraries and advanced topics in graphical user interface design
Hafta 10: Recursion, sorting and searching (selection/merge/binary search)
Hafta 11: Java Collections Framework: lists, stacks, queues and priority queues, sets and maps
Hafta 12: Implementation of lists, stacks, queues and priority queues
Hafta 13: Java web development and introduction to Java Enterprise (J2EE)

courses.name: Probability and Random Variables
courses.purpose: This course is an introductory course on probability and random variables for engineering undergraduate students, especially for third-year computer engineering students. It first presents the fundamental differences between statistics and probability, then focuses on probability concepts. Throughout the semester, probability axioms, probability density functions, joint density functions, random variables, and related topics are covered.
courses.description: This course covers course information, introduction to probability and statistics, basic probability, conditional probability, discrete random variables, discrete distributions and their statistics, continuous random variables and their statistics, continuation of continuous random variables, review of topics for the exam week, continuous distributions, multiple discrete random variables, multiple continuous random variables, conditional discrete probability distribution function, conditional continuous probability distribution function, and conditional continuous probability distribution function.
learning_outcomes: Model simple probability events mathematically.
Determine the probabilities of events in a given sample space and calculate expected value, standard deviation, and conditional probability using random variable modeling.
Develop mathematical models for discrete and continuous random variables.
Determine in which situations the most commonly used probability distributions should be applied.

Model a new random variable depending on multiple random variables and obtain its joint probability density function, conditional probability functions, and transformations of random variables in one and two dimensions.

courses.name: YAZ STAJI I
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, Mühendislik ve Doğa Bilimleri Fakültesi'nde öğrenim gören öğrencilerin staj süreçlerinin takibini sağlamaktır.
courses.description: Bu ders; Staj Bilgilendirme Toplantısı, Staj Başvuru Formlarının Toplanması, Staj Dönemi, staj raporlarının toplanması ve değerlendirilmesi konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Edinilen teorik ve uygulamalı ders kazanımlarını bir araya getirir.
Elde edinilen deneyimlerle, benzer problemlere çözüm bulur.

courses.name: Operating Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce and discuss the concepts, structures, and mechanisms of modern operating systems. It focuses on operating system internals, methods, and algorithms rather than user interfaces.
courses.description: This course covers an overview of computer systems and operating systems, OS services, process definition and control, threads, concurrency, mutual exclusion and synchronization, deadlock and starvation, memory management, virtual memory, single-processor and multiprocessor scheduling, I/O management, disk scheduling, file management, operating system security, virtual machines and cloud operating systems, and mobile, embedded, and IoT operating systems.
learning_outcomes: Understand the concepts, structures, and mechanisms of modern operating systems.
Understand concurrency problems, mutual exclusion, synchronization, deadlock, deadlock prevention, avoidance, and semaphore usage.
Understand memory management, virtual memory, swapping, paging algorithms, and segmentation.
Understand input/output and file management systems.
Learn recent trends in operating systems and developments related to the use of operating systems in virtual machines, cloud systems, mobile, and embedded systems.
Learn the special design decisions and functions of various operating systems such as Unix, Linux, Windows, and Android.

courses.name: Numerical Methods
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a solid foundation in numerical methods used to solve engineering and computational problems that cannot be handled analytically. It develops an understanding of error sources and propagation in numerical computations and introduces systematic techniques for solving nonlinear equations, performing optimization, data fitting and interpolation, and approximating derivatives and integrals. The course emphasizes algorithmic formulation, convergence and stability analysis, and the practical application of numerical methods relevant to computer engineering.
courses.description: Introduction and error analysis in numerical methods, including the role of numerical methods in computer engineering and the effects of rounding and truncation errors; root-finding methods for nonlinear equations such as bracketing methods (bisection, false position) and open methods (Newton-Raphson, secant) with convergence analysis; optimization methods including unconstrained one-dimensional and multidimensional optimization and constrained optimization; curve fitting and interpolation using least squares regression and polynomial/spline interpolation; numerical integration techniques such as finite differences, trapezoidal and Simpson rules, and adaptive quadrature; numerical differentiation using high-accuracy differentiation formulas and Richardson extrapolation.
learning_outcomes: Explain the role and importance of numerical methods in computer engineering and analyze the sources and effects of rounding and truncation errors in numerical computations.
Use Taylor series approximations to improve and analyze numerical algorithms in terms of accuracy and stability.
Apply and compare bracketing and open methods for solving nonlinear equations and evaluate their convergence properties.
Solve one-dimensional and multidimensional optimization problems using appropriate numerical techniques, including constrained and unconstrained methods.
Perform curve fitting and interpolation with least squares regression and polynomial or spline-based approaches, and evaluate their suitability for given data sets.
Approximately compute definite integrals using numerical integration techniques such as finite differences, trapezoidal and Simpson rules, and adaptive quadrature methods.
Compute numerical derivatives using high-accuracy differentiation formulas and improve results through Richardson extrapolation.
Select and apply appropriate numerical methods to practical engineering problems by considering accuracy, efficiency, and computational cost.

courses.name: Data Communication and Computer Networks
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the principles and protocols of computer networks through a top-down network architecture approach, supported by practical Ethereal lab applications. It also aims to show the relationship between OSI layers and how each layer addresses subsets of computer networking requirements.
courses.description: This course covers computer networks and the Internet; application layer principles and protocols such as Web, HTTP, FTP, SMTP, DNS, P2P, and socket programming with TCP and UDP; transport layer services, multiplexing/demultiplexing, UDP, reliable data transfer, TCP, and congestion control; network layer topics including virtual circuit and datagram networks, routers, IP, routing algorithms, RIP, OSPF, BGP, broadcast and multicast routing; link layer topics such as error detection/correction, multiple access, link-layer addressing, Ethernet, switches, PPP, ATM, and MPLS; and wireless and mobile networks including wireless links, IEEE 802.11, and an overview of cellular systems.
learning_outcomes: Explains OSI layers, network, ISP, and Internet concepts.
Learns and analyzes application layer protocols.
Learns and analyzes transport layer protocols.
Explains IP addressing, subnetting, routing, and routing algorithm concepts.
Explains MAC addressing and learns and analyzes link layer protocols.
Explains wireless medium access protocols.

courses.name: PROGRAMLAMA DİLLERİNİN TEMELLERİ
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı genel olarak programlama dillerinin özelliklerini incelemek, programlama dillerinin sözdizimini ve anlambilimini öğrenmek, tüm diller için ortak olan temel yapıları öğrenmek, bu yapıların bazılarını ve belirli diller için olan kavramları incelemek, ana paradigmalarını tanıtmak, bu paradigmaların temsil edildiği bazı dilleri incelemek, çeşitli programlama dili yapıları gerçekleştirme teknikleri öğrenmek, programlama dillerinin belirtimi ile ilgili kavramları çalışmaktır.
courses.description: Bu ders; giriş, önemli dillerin tarihçesi ve gelişimi, sözdizimi ve anlam yapılarının tanımlanması, sözlüksel ve sözdizimi analizi, değişken isimleri, bağlantılar, tip kontrolü ve değişken kapsamları, veri tipleri, deyimler ve atama ifadeleri, sınav haftası, statement-level control structures and subprograms, altprogramların gerçekleştirilmesi, soyut veri tipleri ve kapsülleme yapıları, nesneye yönelik programlama desteği, eşzamanlı çalışma, istisnai durum ve olay işleme, fonksiyonel ve mantıksal programlama dilleri konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Sözdizimi, anlam belirtimi, ve sözcüksel ve sözdizimi analizini anlama
Programlama dillerinin evrimini ve prosedürel, nesne yönelimli, işlevsel, bildirimsel, betik dillerinin rolünü anlamak ve tartışmak.
Emirsel dillerin birincil yapıları hakkındaki tasarım konularını ayrıntılı olarak tanımlama
Kontrol ifadelerini, alt programları ve bu yapıların nasıl gerçekleştirildiğini anlamak ve tartışabilmek, nesne yönelimli dillerin ilkelerini ve yapılarını tanımlamak.
Bir uygulama alanı için gerekli yeni dilleri kolayca öğrenme ve seçebilme becerisine sahip olmak.
Yeni bir dilin tasarımına dahil edilmesi gereken özellikler hakkında fikir sahibi olmak.

courses.name: Microprocessors
courses.purpose: This course aims to evaluate microprocessor architectures and the fundamentals of microprocessor-based system design.
courses.description: This course covers number systems, computer structure and memory, memory design, central processing unit structure and instruction set, addressing methods, instructions I and II, parallel communication interface, serial communication interface, subprograms, interrupts, stack, software applications, and microprocessor-based system development.
learning_outcomes: Discuss the operating principles of microprocessors.
Design digital systems using a microprocessor.
Design real-time systems.
Design systems using the input-output interface.
Design systems using interrupts in microprocessors.

courses.name: Innovation and Entrepreneurship
courses.purpose: To explain the basic concepts related to innovation and entrepreneurship, interpret prominent creative thinking techniques, design innovative business models, explain concepts related to intellectual property, analyze and interpret the successful and unsuccessful aspects of any entrepreneurship story, and complete an application form to obtain entrepreneurship support.
courses.description: This course covers the introduction to entrepreneurship and the assessment of entrepreneurship potential; the role of innovation and innovation management in business life; creative thinking techniques and exercises for developing innovative business ideas; the concept and components of a business plan; business concepts, functions, types, establishment forms, and legal and financial responsibilities; grants and application procedures for techno-startups in Turkey; intellectual property rights and management of intellectual capital; company establishment, capital acquisition, partnership and growth scenarios within the national entrepreneurship support ecosystem; how to prepare an engineering project proposal for the 2209A/B Grant Program; project management tools (decision support systems); literature review tools (e.g., Mendeley); plagiarism; YÖK Scientific Research and Publication Ethics Directive; and individual/group presentations and studies on success and failure stories, entrepreneurship business plans, and research projects.
learning_outcomes: Explains common concepts related to technology, innovation, and entrepreneurship.
Identifies national research funds and develops a proposal project.
Uses creative thinking methods and explains brainstorming, lateral thinking, morphological analysis, La Sella matrices, and TRIZ.
Distinguishes basic terminology such as copyright, trademark, patent, utility model, industrial design, and geographical indication.
Explains the main components of a business plan such as market research, management plan, production plan, marketing plan, and financial plan.

Can work effectively in intra-disciplinary and multidisciplinary teams, transform a scientific research proposal into an original project with students from different programs, and present it in written and oral form.

courses.name: Electric Circuits
courses.purpose: To analyze and design linear electric circuits composed of resistors, capacitors, inductors, and operational amplifiers.
courses.description: This course covers basic circuit concepts (charge, direct and alternating current, voltage, power, energy, circuit elements), basic laws (Ohm's law, Kirchhoff's laws, series/parallel resistor connections), analysis methods (nodal analysis, mesh analysis), circuit theorems (superposition, Thevenin, Norton), operational amplifiers (ideal op-amp, inverting, non-inverting, summing, and differential amplifiers), capacitors and inductors, first-order circuits, and second-order circuits.
learning_outcomes: Students can apply the basic laws of electric circuits.
Students can analyze direct current electric circuits.
Students can explain the behavior of direct current operational amplifier circuits.
Students can formulate the behavior of circuits containing inductors and capacitors.
Students can analyze first- and second-order circuits.
Students can design first- and second-order circuits.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Basic circuit concepts (charge, direct and alternating current, voltage, power, energy, circuit elements)
Hafta 2: Basic laws (Ohm's law, Kirchhoff's laws, series/parallel resistor connections)
Hafta 3: Analysis methods (nodal analysis, mesh analysis)
Hafta 4: Circuit theorems 1 (superposition, Thevenin, Norton theorems)
Hafta 5: Circuit theorems 2 (superposition, Thevenin, Norton theorems)
Hafta 6: Operational amplifiers 1 (ideal op-amp, inverting amplifier, non-inverting amplifier, summing amplifier, differential amplifier)
Hafta 7: Operational amplifiers 2 (ideal op-amp, inverting amplifier, non-inverting amplifier, summing amplifier, differential amplifier)
Hafta 8: Capacitors and inductors 1 (series and parallel connections, integrator, differentiator)
Hafta 9: Capacitors and inductors 2 (series and parallel connections, integrator, differentiator)
Hafta 10: First-order circuits 1 (source-free R-C and R-L circuits, impulse response of R-C and R-L circuits, first-order op-amp circuits)
Hafta 11: First-order circuits 2 (source-free R-C and R-L circuits, impulse response of R-C and R-L circuits, first-order op-amp circuits)
Hafta 12: Second-order circuits 1 (source-free R-L-C circuits, impulse response of R-L-C circuits, general second-order circuits, second-order op-amp circuits)
Hafta 13: Second-order circuits 2 (source-free R-L-C circuits, impulse response of R-L-C circuits, general second-order circuits, second-order op-amp circuits)
Hafta 14: Second-order circuits 3 (source-free R-L-C circuits, impulse response of R-L-C circuits, general second-order circuits, second-order op-amp circuits)

courses.name: Robotics and Intelligent Systems
courses.purpose: This course provides a broad overview of the design, development, and applications of robotic systems. It introduces the historical background and fundamental components of robotics, then builds the theoretical foundation in kinematics, dynamics, control systems, and sensors. The course also aims to equip students with the ability to integrate artificial intelligence and machine learning into robotic systems and to understand key aspects of intelligent systems, human-robot interaction, and real-world applications.
courses.description: This course covers the definition of robotics and intelligent systems, a brief history of robotics, current trends, and an overview of applications. It includes robot components and types, rotation operators, homogeneous transformations, forward and inverse kinematics, velocity kinematics, Newton-Euler dynamics, inertial properties, recursive Newton-Euler algorithms, joint force and torque analysis, Euler-Lagrange equations, and constrained dynamics. The course also introduces AI applications in robotics, sampling theorem, representation theory, medical signal processing, feature extraction methods, computer vision, image filtering and filter design, special filters for image processing, learning types, linear and nonlinear classifiers, and validation methods for small data analysis.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes the fundamental principles of robotics, its main components, their roles in robotic systems, and the history of robotics including major milestones and breakthrough developments.
Applies rotation operators and homogeneous transformations to represent the position and orientation of a robotic end-effector in a unified mathematical framework.

Applies forward kinematics to determine the position of a robot end-effector from joint variables, inverse kinematics to compute the required joint variables for a desired end-effector pose, and velocity kinematics to analyze the relationship between joint velocities and end-effector velocities.

Solves joint force/torque equations by applying inertial properties such as mass, center of gravity, and inertia tensor for rigid bodies in a robotic system, and by using the recursive Newton-Euler algorithm to compute velocities and accelerations in a robotic manipulator.
Derives the equations of motion of robotic systems using the Euler-Lagrange method.
Determines the appropriate learning type, method, and data collection features to build an intelligent system.
Identifies data features in time, frequency, and time-frequency domains using various methods.

courses.name: Yapay Sinir Ağları
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı hesaplamalı nöron modellerinin makine öğrenmesinde ve sinir sistem bileşenlerinin modellenmesinde kullanımını değerlendirmektir.
courses.description: Bu ders; Sinir Sistemi: Mikroskopik Görünüm, Sinir Sistemi: Makroskopik Görünüm, Makine Öğrenmesi, Algılayıcı, Çok katmanlı algılayıcı, Gözetimli Öğrenme, Geri Yayılım Algoritması, Çevrimiçi Öğrenme, Yığın Öğrenme, Aşırı uyum, Desen Sınıflandırma için Sinir Ağları, Bağlanımda Sinir Ağları, Nöromodülasyon, Pekiştirmeli Öğrenme konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Tek katmanlı algılayıcı tasarlar.
Çevrimiçi öğrenme algoritması gerçekler.
Çok katmanlı algılayıcı kullanarak sınıflandırıcılar geliştirebilir.
Regresyon için çok katmanlı sinir ağları tasarlar.

courses.name: DEVOPS
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the fundamental knowledge and practical skills required for Linux system administration. Through a combination of theoretical concepts and hands-on practice, students learn to use basic Linux commands effectively, monitor system performance, manage logs, and work with container technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes. The course also emphasizes security best practices and automation in system administration to prepare students for real-world challenges and rapidly evolving technologies.
courses.description: This course covers an overview of Linux operating systems, understanding the Linux file system, introduction to the shell and shell commands, user and group management, package management, system updates and upgrades, monitoring Linux systems using tools such as top, ps, and iostat, monitoring software, log management, understanding system logs, using logrotate, ELK and Grafana dashboards, process management, monitoring system performance with top, ps, and other tools, file system management, creating, mounting, and unmounting file systems, understanding file system permissions and security, overview of Docker, installing and configuring Docker, creating and running Docker containers, Docker images, using Dockerfiles to build images, deploying multiple applications, midterm exam, introduction to Kubernetes, overview of Kubernetes, understanding nodes and pods, deploying applications with Kubernetes, understanding Kubernetes services and load balancing, configuring storage and volumes, overview of Kubernetes security, advanced Kubernetes topics including replication controllers and stateful sets, introduction to configuration, using Kubernetes with Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD), Linux scripting, automating system administration tasks, Linux performance optimization, understanding CPU and memory usage, optimizing performance with tuning, project presentation, applying the concepts covered in the course to real-world scenarios, and final exam.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrates proficiency in using basic Linux commands.
Effectively monitors system performance and resource usage.
Has the necessary knowledge to create, manage, and deploy Docker containers to servers.
Understands the principles of containerization and explains the basic concepts of Kubernetes architecture and components.
Applies learned concepts through practical projects and labs in real-world scenarios.
Gains experience in troubleshooting and resolving common issues in Linux system administration, containerization, and orchestration.

courses.name: Introduction to Graph Theory and Machine Learning in Neuroscience
courses.description: Bu ders; ; konularını içermektedir.

courses.name: Introduction to Machine Learning
courses.purpose: To enable students to apply and evaluate machine learning techniques.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamentals of machine learning, regression, basics of classification, Bayes classifier, logistic regression, support vector machines, neural networks, convolutional neural networks, decision trees, ensemble methods, feature selection, principal component analysis, clustering, and model evaluation.
learning_outcomes: Applies regression techniques
Evaluates classification techniques
Applies unsupervised machine learning
Applies feature selection / analysis techniques

courses.name: ELEKTRONİK I
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce semiconductor theory and electronic circuit elements and to evaluate their applications in basic circuits.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to the course and amplifiers, a summary of PN junctions, diode circuits such as rectifier circuits, limiters and voltage multipliers, a summary of BJT and MOS physics, BJT and MOSFET AC models, transistor DC biasing, resistor degeneration and current sources, single-transistor amplifiers, current sources and cascode amplifiers, differential amplifiers, active loads and OPAMPs, telescopic and folded opamps, continuation of opamps, introduction to frequency response, and frequency response with a summary for the final exam.
learning_outcomes: Has basic knowledge about semiconductor physics and the structure of the PN junction.
Understands the operating principles of diodes and knows how to analyze and design regulator, rectifier, limiter, and clipper circuits.
Performs analysis and design of amplifier circuits with MOS devices and one- and two-transistor configurations.
Designs and analyzes current source, current mirror, and active load circuits.
Analyzes and designs differential amplifier circuits.
Understands the physical structure and operating principles of MOS transistors and knows how to bias them in circuits.

courses.name: Signals and Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the representation and characteristics of continuous-time and discrete-time signals, the definition of systems and especially the characteristics and mathematical representations of linear and time-invariant systems, and their analysis in time and frequency domains. It also aims to enable students to understand convolution, Fourier series and transforms, Laplace transform and its use in system analysis, and Z-transform for discrete-time systems. Matlab will be used to support problem solving.
