courses.description: This course covers introduction to course information and signals, basic signals, continuous-time systems, convolution, Fourier series for continuous time, Fourier transform for continuous time, Laplace transform, applications of Fourier transform to signals and systems, applications of Laplace transform to signals and systems, discrete-time signals, discrete-time systems, Fourier series and transform for discrete time, discrete-time Fourier transform, and Z-transform.
learning_outcomes: Understand the representation and characteristics of continuous-time and discrete-time signals.
Be able to analyze the definition of systems, especially linear and time-invariant systems, and their mathematical representations in time and frequency domains.
Acquire the ability to realize system output signals by convolution.
Develop the ability to use Fourier series and transforms appropriately.
Learn the use of Laplace transform in system analysis.
Understand the use of Z-transform in the analysis of discrete-time systems.

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Vision
courses.purpose: To understand the fundamental topics in the field of computer vision and to apply and evaluate various computer vision techniques.
courses.description: This course covers optical image formation, imaging stages, image filtering, edge detection and Hough transform, morphological operations, image enhancement, keypoint extraction (basic ideas), keypoint extraction (scale-invariant methods), image interpolation, geometric transformations, motion estimation, camera calibration, 3D imaging, and color space.
learning_outcomes: Understand and apply basic image processing techniques
Understand and apply image formation and modeling concepts
Understand and apply various mid-level computer vision techniques such as feature extraction and optical flow
Design and evaluate solutions to computer vision problems

courses.name: Project Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip engineering students with effective project management skills in a rapidly changing technological and highly competitive modern world. It teaches how to manage project design and implementation from both engineering and management perspectives, and helps students develop the ability to plan, monitor, and complete complex projects effectively.
courses.description: This course covers definitions and concepts related to project management, project selection methods and techniques, organizational structure, company culture and project management, project definition, estimation of project duration and costs, development of project plans, project risk management, project resource and cost planning, project crashing, workforce planning and management, use of external resources for projects and international project management, project monitoring, performance evaluation, project closure, and agile project management.
learning_outcomes: Defines the strategic importance of project management in today's business environment and special topics related to project management such as agile project management, Scrum, international project management, and virtual project teams.
Defines project scope and applies analytical tools to select among different project alternatives.
Defines a project management plan for cost, schedule, and project team, and evaluates risks.
Evaluates basic project performance measures and defines project closure activities.
Defines project success and failure factors through case studies.
Uses project management software.

courses.name: Algorithm Analysis
courses.purpose: To introduce fundamental algorithm design techniques for solving computational problems related to both applied and theoretical computer science, and to examine techniques used to analyze the performance of algorithms developed with these methods. Mathematical foundations for algorithm analysis will be covered for sorting, searching, graph problems, and geometric problems.
courses.description: This course covers algorithm analysis, asymptotic notation, divide-and-conquer methods, solving recurrences, analysis of quicksort and randomized quicksort, heapsort, linear-time sorting, order statistics, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, amortized analysis, dynamic tables, graphs, and breadth-first search (BFS).
learning_outcomes: At the end of this course, the student will be able to explain the main principles of algorithm analysis and designing new algorithms.
At the end of this course, the student will be able to construct more complex algorithms using basic data structures learned previously.
At the end of this course, the student will be able to develop complex algorithms using trees and apply these algorithms and data structures to real-world problems.
At the end of this course, the student will be able to develop complex algorithms using graphs and apply these algorithms and data structures to real-world problems.

At the end of this course, the student will be able to systematically approach a new computational problem and formulate original algorithms and data structures for its solution using methods such as dynamic programming and divide-and-conquer.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to algorithm analysis and explanation of course-related information
Hafta 5: Analysis of quicksort, randomized quicksort

courses.name: DATABASES
courses.purpose: To explain the design and programming concepts underlying database systems. Using relational database systems as a model, the course aims to build a foundation in data management and put this foundation into practice by designing a real database application.
courses.description: This course covers Database Management Systems, Relational Database Systems, entity and relationship model, relational algebra, Structured Query Language (SQL), database application development, relational database design, file organization, tree data structure indexing, command-based indexing, query processing, query optimization, concurrency control, and a summary of transaction processing systems.
learning_outcomes: At the end of this course, the student will be able to explain and discuss the basic methods and algorithms required for database management systems.
At the end of this course, the student will be able to formulate real-life database problems using relational data structure methods and solve these problems with the correct methods.
At the end of this course, the student will learn the data structures that constitute data structures and the algorithms that use these data structures.
Within the scope of this course, the student will learn how to interact with database applications using the SQL query language.

courses.name: Microprocessors
courses.purpose: This course aims to evaluate microprocessor architectures and the fundamentals of microprocessor-based system design.
courses.description: This course covers number systems, computer structure and memory, memory design, central processing unit structure and instruction set, addressing methods, instructions I, instructions II, parallel communication interface, serial communication interface, subroutines, interrupts, stack, software applications, and microprocessor-based system development.
learning_outcomes: Discuss the operating principles of microprocessors.
Design digital systems using a microprocessor.
Design real-time systems.
Design systems using the input-output interface.
Design systems using interrupts in microprocessors.

courses.name: WEB PROGRAMLAMA
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop websites with standards that consider both client-side and server-side programming. Students will learn how the internet works, web standards, web design concepts, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PHP, and will gain practical experience through a semester project.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to web design, how the web works, HTML introduction, text markup, adding links, adding images, table markup, forms, embedded media, introduction to CSS, text formatting, colors and backgrounds, box model concepts, floating and positioning, CSS layout with Flexbox, responsive web design, transitions, transformations and animation, introduction to JavaScript, using JavaScript and the Document Object Model, advanced JavaScript programming, midterm project presentation, PHP basics and introduction to XAMPP, PHP forms and advanced topics, introduction to PhpMyAdmin, MySQL and basic SQL statements, advanced SQL statements, and final project presentation.
learning_outcomes: Explains how the client-server model works in internet programming
Designs interactive, client-side, executable web applications
Demonstrates how internet programming tasks are performed
Recognizes the languages of the web: HTML and CSS
Gains practical experience in web development on both the client side and the server side

courses.name: Advanced Robotics
courses.purpose: This course covers robot motion, control systems, sensor technologies, and coordination. It provides students with a comprehensive understanding of robot dynamics and control by including advanced topics such as differential kinematics, path planning, and trajectory generation. The course also introduces state-of-the-art control techniques such as force control, impedance control, and admittance control, while giving students practical experience in robot software development using programming languages required for advanced applications.
courses.description: This course includes an introduction to robotic systems, their components and types; analysis of rotation operators to describe and control the orientation of robotic end-effectors; homogeneous transformations to represent position and orientation in a unified mathematical framework; solving the forward kinematics problem to determine the end-effector position given joint variables; solving inverse kinematics problems to compute the joint variables required to reach a desired end-effector position and orientation; the concept of velocity kinematics and analysis of the relationship between joint velocities and end-effector velocities in a robotic system; derivation of robot motion equations using the Newton-Euler method, including calculation of inertial properties such as mass, center of mass, and moment of inertia for individual rigid bodies in a robotic system; computation of velocities and accelerations in a robotic manipulator using the recursive Newton-Euler algorithm; analysis of joint forces and torques and expressing them in terms of external forces, joint accelerations, and inertial properties; performing dynamic simulations of robotic manipulators using the Newton-Euler method; derivation of Lagrange equations to express the dynamics of mechanical systems; solving dynamic problems with constraints using Euler-Lagrange equations; understanding the principles of force control in robotics; exploring the role of force sensors and tactile feedback in robot systems; analyzing the challenges and applications of force control in various scenarios; studying adaptive control techniques applicable to robotic systems; examining how adaptive control can improve robot performance in response to changing environmental conditions; implementing real-time feedback mechanisms for force control; and examining the importance of closed-loop control systems in adapting to dynamic changes.
learning_outcomes: Analyze complex aspects of robot motion, including differential kinematics, path planning, and trajectory generation.
Apply force control, impedance control, and admittance control to effectively manage and optimize robotic behavior.
Apply the theoretical background gained in robot dynamics and control to practical scenarios.
Gain practical experience in robot software development using fundamental frameworks and programming languages for advanced applications and system integration.
Apply design principles, including materials and manufacturing methods, to create a prototype of a robotic system.

courses.name: C++ PROGRAMLAMA
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students gain a deep understanding of C++ programming through both fundamental and advanced concepts. It focuses on improving problem-solving skills while developing the ability to write efficient, maintainable, and high-performance C++ programs. The course also provides practical experience with advanced features such as object-oriented programming, memory management, file operations, exception handling, the Standard Template Library (STL), operator overloading, and templates.
courses.description: Introduction to C++, C++ fundamentals, functions and scope, arrays and character arrays, pointers and dynamic memory management, introduction to object-oriented programming, advanced object-oriented programming, inheritance and polymorphism, file handling in C++, exception handling, templates and generic programming, Standard Template Library (STL) I, Standard Template Library (STL) II, and advanced C++ features.
learning_outcomes: Understand C++ syntax and fundamental concepts
Apply object-oriented programming principles
Use memory management techniques
Handle file operations and exceptions
Use templates and the Standard Template Library
Develop and optimize real-world applications

courses.name: Introduction to Deep Learning
courses.purpose: This course introduces deep learning. Deep learning is a branch of machine learning concerned with the development and application of modern neural networks. The course covers fundamental neural networks, convolutional and recurrent architectures, unsupervised deep learning, and applications in areas such as computer vision, image processing, and natural language processing.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to machine learning and neural networks, training of neural networks, convolutional neural networks (CNNs), layers in CNNs, deep learning hardware and software, deep learning architectures, deep learning strategies, computer vision applications, computer vision and deep learning, image processing and deep learning, natural language processing and deep learning, recurrent neural networks and LSTMs, unsupervised learning and generative modeling, and advanced applications of deep learning.
learning_outcomes: Design convolutional neural networks for supervised/unsupervised learning
Analyze the effects of hyper-parameters on learning performance
Apply learning techniques to train deep networks
Recognize applications of deep networks in computer vision, image processing, and natural language processing
Use current software and hardware tools for deep learning

courses.name: ELEKTRONİK II
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce semiconductor theory and electronic circuit elements and to evaluate their applications in basic circuits. It also covers frequency effects in circuit design, feedback, stability, and an introduction to op-amp design.
courses.description: The course covers an introduction to the course, a review of diodes and transistors, a review of simple amplifiers and bias, a review of OPAMP DC characteristics, an introduction to frequency response and Cadence, frequency behavior of integrated circuits, Bode plots and time-constant methods, pole-zero calculation, feedback techniques for integrated circuits, review and assessments, stability and compensation, practical feedback and loading, op-amp design, and project presentations and review.
learning_outcomes: Understanding amplifiers and determining their DC characteristics.
Understanding the frequency behavior of integrated circuits.
Learning pole-zero calculation and time-constant calculation.
Understanding feedback techniques in integrated circuits.
Understanding and identifying circuit stability.
Understanding frequency compensation of circuits.
Understanding op-amp design and compensation.

courses.name: Haberleşme Sistemleri
courses.purpose: Dersin amacı öğrencilere modern haberleşme sistemlerinin temel prensiplerini ve tekniklerini öğretmektir. Dersin içeriğinde haberleşme sistemlerinde işaret ve sistem gösterimleri; sürekli dalgalı modülasyonu (genlik modülasyonu ve açısal modülasyon); modülasyon and demodülasyon teknikleri; işaret iletimi ve kanal gürültüsünün performans üzerinde etkileri; işaret örnekleme; analog ve sayısal darbe modülasyonu; taban bantta darbe genlik modülasyonu; darbe biçimleme ve uyumlu süzgeç bulunmaktadır.
courses.description: Bu ders; Haberleşme sistemlerine giriş ve sinyaller, Sinyaller ve Sistemlerin Gösterimi, Fourier Dönüşümü, Genlik Modülasyonu, Açı Modülasyonu, Olasılık, Rasgele Süreçler, Rasgele Süreçlerin Sistemlerden geçişi, Analogdan Dijitale Dönüşüm, PCM Sistemleri, Temel Bantlı Dijital İletişim, Dijital İletişim Teknolojileri, Dijital Modülasyon: PSK, BFSK ve ASK, modern haberleşme sistemlerine genel bir bakış konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Sinyaller ve sistemleri haberleşme sistemlerine uygular.
Fourier Dönüşümünü haberleşme sistemlerinde etkin bir şekilde kullanır.
Modülasyon konusunda temel teşkil eden analog genlik ve frekans modülasyon kavramlarını simüle eder.
Analog ve sayısal boyutlar arasında dönüşümün sağlayarak bunun ses sinyalleri ile gösterimini gerçekleştirir.
Rastsal süreçlerin sayısal haberleşmeye olan uygulamalarının gösterilmesi.
Sayısal temel bant ve üst bant iletişim sistemleri için kullanılan iletişim tekniklerinin bilgisayar ortamında gösterilmesi.
prerequisites: SİNYALLER VE SİSTEMLER / OLASILIK VE RASSAL DEĞİŞKENLER

courses.name: Introduction to Modeling and Optimization
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach mathematical techniques that help formulate and analyze mathematical models using selected real-life problems, and to solve linear programming and network problems.
courses.description: Introduction to modeling, basic linear algebra review (matrices and vectors, linear equations, Gauss-Jordan method, linear dependence and independence, matrix inverse and determinant), introduction to linear programming, formulation of linear programming models, convex sets and functions, extreme points and optimality, graphical solution, sensitivity analysis in graphical solution and computer-based solution, simplex algorithm, simplex algorithm with artificial initial solutions, simplex algorithm with artificial initial solutions and special cases, revised simplex, special simplex applications: Karush-Kuhn-Tucker optimality conditions, duality and sensitivity, duality and sensitivity: dual simplex method, transportation and assignment problems.
learning_outcomes: The student defines modeling concepts.
The student analyzes mathematical models.
The student formulates problems using linear programming.
The student applies the simplex algorithm.
The student defines duality and sensitivity analysis.
The student solves transportation and assignment models.
prerequisites: LINEER CEBİR / LİNEER CEBİR VE DİFERANSİYEL DENKLEMLER

courses.name: Applied Statistics
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the ability to collect, analyze, and interpret data using basic statistical methods and to apply these skills to engineering problems.
courses.description: Introduction to statistics and data analysis, sampling distributions, estimation, confidence intervals for one sample, hypothesis testing for one sample, confidence intervals for two samples, hypothesis testing for two samples, introduction to correlation and regression analysis, linear regression models, multiple regression models, and advanced topics in multiple regression models.
learning_outcomes: Summarize and interpret data using graphical and/or numerical methods.
Distinguish between population and sample.
Construct confidence intervals for population characteristics.
Construct hypothesis tests for population characteristics.
Apply correlation and linear regression analyses.
Use the SPSS statistical package to apply statistical procedures discussed throughout the term.

courses.name: Project Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip engineering students with effective project management skills in today’s rapidly changing technological and highly competitive environment. It also teaches students to manage project design and implementation from both engineering and management perspectives, integrating engineering system design, modeling, and implementation with project management concepts.
courses.description: This course covers definitions and concepts related to project management, project selection methods and techniques, organizational structure, company culture and project management, project definition, estimation of project duration and costs, development of a project plan, project risk management, project resource and cost planning, project crashing, workforce planning and management, use of external resources for projects and international project management, project monitoring, performance evaluation, project closure, and agile project management.
learning_outcomes: Defines the strategic importance of project management in today’s business environment and special topics related to project management such as agile project management, Scrum, international project management, and virtual project teams.
Defines project scope and applies analytical tools to select among different project alternatives.
Defines a project management plan for cost, schedule, and project team, and evaluates risks.
Evaluates basic project performance measures and defines project closure activities.
Defines project success and failure factors through case studies.
Uses project management software.

courses.name: YAZ STAJI II
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, Mühendislik ve Doğa Bilimleri Fakültesi'nde öğrenim gören öğrencilerin staj süreçlerinin takibini sağlamaktır.
courses.description: Bu ders; Staj Bilgilendirme Toplantısı, Staj Başvuru Formlarının Toplanması, Staj Dönemi, staj raporlarının toplanması ve değerlendirilmesi konularını içermektedir.
learning_outcomes: Elde edinilen deneyimlerle, benzer problemlere çözüm bulur.
Edinilen teorik ve uygulamalı ders kazanımlarını bir araya getirir.

courses.name: Occupational Health and Safety I
courses.purpose: This course covers workplace health and safety topics including occupational health and safety culture, the national occupational health and safety system, legal frameworks, employers' responsibilities in providing safety services, and costs related to work accidents and occupational diseases. It also provides an overview of risk factors affecting safety, factors related to individuals and organizations, and the documents required for occupational health and safety. The course aims to help students understand the historical development of occupational health and safety globally and in Turkey, as well as basic concepts of labor law and international regulations developed by OSHA.
courses.description: Orientation, introduction, causes of accidents, accident causation theories, roles of parties, occupational safety laws and regulations in Turkey, cost of accidents, and the general overview of occupational health and safety in Turkey.
learning_outcomes: Defines occupational health and safety.
Describes the purpose of occupational health and safety.
Draws conclusions from the historical development of occupational health and safety before, during, and after the industrial revolution.
Identifies the causes of accidents.
Relates theories concerning accident causation.
Defines the safety-related roles of each party.
Applies legislation related to occupational safety and health in Turkey.
Identifies laws related to occupational safety and health in other countries.

courses.name: Introduction to Formal Languages and Automata Theory
courses.purpose: This course introduces the concepts of automata theory. It focuses on identifying different classes of formal languages, their relationships, and their differences. Students are expected to design theoretical machines for specific purposes and prove or disprove their properties.
courses.description: This course covers course information, introduction to finite state automata, deterministic and nondeterministic finite state automata, equivalence of deterministic and nondeterministic automata, regular expressions, algebraic laws for regular expressions, pumping lemma for regular languages and minimization of finite state automata, context-free grammars, context-free languages, parse trees and ambiguity of grammars, pushdown automata, Chomsky normal form, pumping lemma for context-free languages, Turing machines, and simple mathematical operations with Turing machines.
learning_outcomes: To define different language classes and design automata to accept these languages
To prove the equivalence of different automata and scientifically show whether a given language is regular
To represent a specific language through context-free grammar, elimination of ambiguity, and simplification of a given grammar
To design a Turing machine for specific purposes

courses.name: MÜHENDİSLİK PROJESİ I
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable engineering students to apply the theoretical knowledge they have gained during their studies to a working practical system. Students work in small groups to design, build, and present an ambitious engineering design project.
courses.description: This course covers project topic selection, system design implementation, project planning, computer-aided design implementation, simulation, semester reporting, and presentation.
learning_outcomes: Understand all requirements of a given project in detail.
Learn the steps required for project design and implementation.
Consider ethical values while carrying out a project.
Develop written and oral communication skills.
Understand the necessity of lifelong learning.
Use modern tools and techniques for a given project.
Demonstrate the perseverance needed to overcome difficult problems.

courses.name: Software Engineering
courses.purpose: This course teaches an engineering approach to developing software systems. Students work in teams to create a software product through stages such as feasibility study, requirements analysis, object-oriented design, implementation, testing, and delivery to the customer. The course also covers advanced topics such as project management and risk analysis.
courses.description: This course covers software development and processes, project planning and organization, software engineering practices and project presentation, requirements engineering, analysis methods, elements of software design, design methods I and II, test strategies, test methods, risk management I and II, software project effort estimation, and final project presentations.
learning_outcomes: Can work on a software engineering project with people having various roles
Creates UML diagrams for completing software engineering projects
Recognizes all fundamental concepts of software engineering
Can follow software engineering principles
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Software Engineering Practices and Project Presentation
Hafta 8: All topics up to the 7th week
Hafta 14: Effort Estimation in Software Projects

courses.name: Mühendislik Projesi II
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable engineering students to apply the theoretical knowledge they have gained throughout their studies to a working system in practice. Students working in small groups design, build, and present an ambitious engineering design project.
courses.description: This course covers identifying the main components of the project, including both hardware components and software platforms; developing the first version of the software part; producing the first prototype of the system; producing the second prototype; integrating all system components and conducting tests; and preparing the semester report and presentation.
learning_outcomes: Develop a prototype using different engineering topics.
The ability to convert theoretical knowledge into practical engineering designs.
Understand that a designed prototype should undergo different tests and be able to create test plans.
Present designs in written, visual, and oral forms.
Understand the concept of project duration and know what should be done to successfully complete the project during busy periods.

courses.name: Engineering Economics
courses.purpose: To introduce the fundamental concepts of economic analysis for engineering and management decisions and explain how these concepts are applied in a firm's or a government's project planning and decision-making process.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to engineering economics, a brief overview of economics and finance, time value of money and economic equivalence, engineering economy factors, nominal and effective rates, project evaluation methods, rate of return determination, solution of multiple rates of return, decision rules in rate of return analysis, benefit-cost analysis, capital budgeting, inflation and index numbers, replacement analysis, and after-tax economic analysis.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental principles of engineering economics and their relationship with engineering applications.
Understand the basic concepts of economics and finance and explain their relationship with engineering economics analyses.
Perform cost estimation using engineering economics terminology.
Calculate the time value of money using engineering economics terminology.
Use present worth and different annual worth techniques to evaluate or select alternatives.
Perform rate of return and incremental rate of return analyses.
Conduct public sector project benefit/cost analyses, use methods for reducing the book value of capital investment, and consider the effects of inflation.

courses.name: Bioinformatics
courses.purpose: This course provides an introduction to the field of bioinformatics, covering fundamental concepts, algorithms, structures, databases, the historical development of the field, its applications, and related developments. It also introduces the basics of bioinformatics sequence analysis and the relevant tools and databases, as well as molecular biology foundations such as genes and genomes.
courses.description: Introduction to course materials; what bioinformatics is and why it should be studied; background and basic concepts in bioinformatics; suffix trees and sequences; basics of sequence alignment; pairwise sequence alignment; multiple sequence alignment; databases and database searching; microarray data analysis; student presentations on course/papers/tools; phylogenetic trees; machine learning; network models and graph analysis; biological networks, visualization and analysis; project presentation.
learning_outcomes: Understand and visualize basic approaches to biological networks.
Can use biological databases and knowledge bases, machine learning, and network analysis.
Can compare the technical aspects of pairwise local and global sequence alignment algorithms.
Can understand and explain the fundamentals of molecular biology and evolution related to sequence alignment and relate them to various algorithms.
Understands dynamic programming algorithms for biological sequence alignment.
Has a general understanding of central topics and concepts in bioinformatics.

courses.name: Data Science
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamentals of data science, a rapidly evolving field that is highly relevant for both researchers and practitioners in the 21st century. It aims to equip students with the core skills of a data scientist and enable them to apply these skills in areas such as medicine, defense industry, engineering, and finance.
courses.description: This course provides a general introduction to data science, probability, statistics, and linear algebra. It covers basic data models, relational models, SQL, the transition from SQL to NoSQL, non-relational databases and related data models such as XML and XQuery, MongoDB, sources and types of big data, frequent pattern analysis, clustering, classification, incremental data analysis, scalable methods for data management and analysis, network models and graph analysis, data visualization, and recommender systems.
learning_outcomes: Understanding the fundamentals of data science and the different skills a data scientist should possess.
Understanding the basics of data collection, data modeling, and data management within data science processes.
Understanding fundamental statistical modeling and analysis in data science processes.
Understanding machine learning algorithms and techniques used in data science tasks.
Understanding basic network modeling and graph analysis that can be used in data science tasks.
Understanding basic approaches to data presentation or visualization and the importance of data presentation for basic communication.

courses.name: Introduction to Information Security
courses.purpose: To understand the actors in the cybersecurity world and the motivations of cybercriminals and cybersecurity professionals. To learn how to identify security attacks, their indicators, processes, and countermeasures. To develop skills in security management, controls, protection, and mitigation technologies, and to understand security laws, ethics, and policy development as well as career roles in cybersecurity.
courses.description: This course covers the cybersecurity world and its importance, attacks, concepts and techniques, protecting data, privacy and organizations, cybersecurity threats, vulnerabilities and attacks, the art of protecting secrets and ensuring integrity, protecting the cybersecurity domain, the Five Nines concept, cybersecurity frameworks, security life cycles, physical and environmental security, disaster recovery and business continuity management, review of all topics covered, and project presentations.
learning_outcomes: Explain how the principles of confidentiality, integrity, and availability are related to data states and cybersecurity measures.
Explain the tactics, techniques, and procedures used by cybercriminals.
Explain how technologies, products, and procedures are used to protect confidentiality and ensure integrity.
Explain how cybersecurity professionals use technologies, processes, and procedures to defend all components of a network.
Explain the purpose of laws related to cybersecurity.
Explain the international information security standard ISO/IEC 27001: Information Security Management System.

courses.name: Introduction to Biometric Systems
courses.description: This course covers the topics of biometric systems.

courses.name: Special Topics in Computer Engineering
courses.description: Bu ders; ; konularını içermektedir.

courses.name: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce and teach the fundamentals of artificial intelligence problems, theories, algorithms, and applications. It focuses on intelligent systems that are expected to have a major impact on industry, the economy, and social life.
courses.description: This course covers the definition and history of AI, problem solving by search, game playing, knowledge representation, propositional logic, first-order predicate logic, logical and probabilistic reasoning, planning, uncertain knowledge and reasoning, machine learning (popular machine learning algorithms, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and genetic algorithms), natural language processing, deep learning for NLP, computer vision, and robotics.
learning_outcomes: Students will have an in-depth understanding of the fundamental areas of artificial intelligence.
Students will learn and understand various search methods, knowledge representation, uncertainty, reasoning, machine learning, natural language processing, computer vision, and robotics.
Students will be able to select an appropriate algorithm to solve an artificial intelligence problem.
Students will be introduced to current research in artificial intelligence and encouraged to define research problems and develop effective solutions.

courses.name: YAZ STAJI II
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, Mühendislik ve Doğa Bilimleri Fakültesi'nde öğrenim gören öğrencilerin staj süreçlerinin takibini sağlamaktır.
courses.description: Bu ders; Staj Bilgilendirme Toplantısı, Staj Basvuru Formlarının Toplanması, Staj Dönemi, Staj raporlarının toplanması ve değerlendirilmesi konularını içermektedir.

courses.name: İş Sağlığı ve Güvenliği II
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide participants with comprehensive knowledge about workplace safety hazards and equip them with the skills and strategies needed to prevent and reduce these hazards. By combining theoretical understanding with practical applications, participants will be prepared to build and maintain a safety culture in the workplace.
courses.description: This course covers orientation, introduction, safety training and instruction, mechanical hazards and machine guarding, fall, impact, acceleration, lifting and vision hazards with appropriate PPE, excessive temperature hazards, pressure hazards, electrical hazards, fire hazards and life safety, noise and vibration hazards, and industrial hygiene and confined spaces.
learning_outcomes: Describe the importance of safety training and its contribution to hazard prevention.
Describe the principles and techniques of machine guarding for injury prevention.
Recognize and evaluate risks related to fall, impact, acceleration, lifting, and vision hazards.
Apply strategies for managing temperature-related risks in the workplace.
Outline safety measures and best practices for addressing pressure-related risks.
Demonstrate knowledge of safe electrical work practices.
Describe the importance of life safety measures in fire situations.
Understand the risks associated with confined spaces and safe entry and work procedures.

courses.name: Engineering Project I
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable engineering students to apply the theoretical knowledge they have acquired throughout their studies to a working practical system. Students work in small groups to design, implement, and present an ambitious engineering design project.
courses.description: This course covers project topic selection, system design, project planning, computer-based design implementation, simulation, and semester report preparation and presentation.
learning_outcomes: Understand all requirements of a given project in detail.
Learn the steps required for project design and implementation.
Consider ethical values while carrying out a project.
Develop written and oral communication skills.
Understand the necessity of lifelong learning.
Use modern tools and techniques for a given project.
Demonstrate the perseverance needed to overcome difficult problems.

courses.name: Mühendislik Projesi II
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable engineering students to apply the theoretical knowledge they have gained throughout their studies to a working practical system. Students working in small groups design, build, and present an ambitious engineering design project.
courses.description: This course covers identifying the main components of the project, including both hardware components and software platforms; developing the first version of the software part; producing the first prototype of the system; producing the second prototype; integrating all system components and conducting tests; and preparing the semester report and presentation.
learning_outcomes: Developing a prototype using different engineering topics.
The ability to convert theoretical knowledge into practical engineering designs.
Understanding that a designed prototype should undergo different tests and being able to create test plans.
Written, visual, and oral presentation of designs.
Understanding the concept of project duration and knowing what should be done to successfully complete the project during busy periods.

courses.name: TECHNOLOGY, SOCIETY AND ETHICS
courses.purpose: This course aims to convey the historical development of science and technology throughout history, explain with concrete examples how political will, social philosophy, financial support, incentives, and stability affect the development of science and technology, and show that the welfare and independence of society can only be sustained through contemporary science and advanced technology. It also aims to help students establish the connections between modern science and technology, strong professional competence, and a successful economy in global competition.
courses.description: This course covers professional ethics; science before science in Mesopotamia and Egypt; scientific method and lifelong learning; the land of the Greeks; research concept; debates in plagiarism and sustainable development; the roads to Baghdad; safety, risk and design; engineering standards and Abbasid Baghdad: The House of Wisdom; research models and social responsibility; spiritual physick; cross-cultural engineering ethics; from Baghdad to Central Asia; data collection and ethical leadership; the cure of ignorance; intellectual property and innovation; Fatimid Cairo: The science of light; professional responsibility; Ayyubid and Mamluk Cairo: healing; ingenious mechanical devices and sustainable engineering; workplace cultures, responsibilities and rights; Islamic technology; cross-cultural collaboration in engineering; Al-Andalus; engineering and environmental ethics in modern context and lifelong learning; and student presentations.
learning_outcomes: The student learns the historical development of science and technology throughout history.
The student understands with concrete examples the effects of political will, social philosophy, financial support, incentives, and stability on the development of science and technology.
The student sees that the welfare and independence of society can only be sustained through contemporary science and advanced technology.
The student can establish the connections between contemporary science and technology, strong professional competence, and a successful economy in global competition.
The student recognizes scientific research techniques.
The student evaluates the ethical rules in the publication process of scientific articles.
The student plans the process of preparing a research project.
The student applies ethical rules in scientific research and project preparation processes.
The student summarizes intellectual property rights.

courses.name: Biosensors
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental engineering principles used to detect small molecules, DNA, proteins, and cells in the context of diagnostic tests, pharmaceutical research, and environmental monitoring. It also covers biosensor approaches such as electrochemical, fluorescence, acoustic, and optical methods, along with selective surface chemistry, biosensor performance characterization, and commercial biosensor case studies.
courses.description: This course covers introduction to biosensors, biological biosensor elements, immobilization of biological elements, electrochemical transducers, optical transducers, piezoelectric transducers, immunosensors, performance parameters, lab-on-a-chip biosensors, nanobiosensors, biosensor applications, applications of biosensors-II, flexible and stretchable bioelectronics-I, and flexible and stretchable bioelectronics-II.
learning_outcomes: Defines the fundamental concepts underlying the operation of major biosensor groups.
Recognizes the characterization, comparison, and application-oriented design of biosensors.
Evaluates the interaction of biochemical functionality with biosensor transducers.
Recognizes biosensor applications in diagnostic tests, life sciences research, and environmental monitoring.
Recognizes newly developed important biosensor systems.
Gains a critical mindset in the development of new diagnostic technologies.

courses.name: Tıbbi Görüntüleme
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental terminology of medical imaging techniques and the physical principles on which they are based, together with the necessary engineering knowledge related to device hardware. It also aims to help students understand clinical engineering applications for therapeutic purposes using imaging data.
courses.description: This course covers the structure of the atom, radioactivity, Rayleigh scattering, Compton scattering and the photoelectric effect; X-rays, X-ray production, X-ray tubes, radiography devices, fluoroscopy and angiography; computed tomography devices and their basic operating principles; sound waves and acoustics, reflection, refraction, scattering and attenuation, piezoelectric effect, transducers, ultrasonography, ultrasound modes and acquisition; proton, spin, magnetic moment, electromagnetism, magnetic field and radio frequency, MRI devices, image formation in MRI, functional MRI and the BOLD technique, diffusion tensor MRI, nuclear medicine imaging, positron emission tomography and its operating principles, three-dimensional reconstruction tools and digital twin creation, image analysis with MATLAB, and medical image processing with MATLAB.
learning_outcomes: Classifies the techniques used in medical imaging.
Evaluates the physical principles on which imaging techniques operate.
Evaluates information about the operating principles and hardware of imaging devices.
Identifies the usage areas, advantages, and disadvantages of devices.
Applies image formation methods using MATLAB.

courses.name: MOBILE DEVICE APPLICATION DEVELOPMENT
courses.purpose: This course provides hands-on experience in building cross-platform applications using the Dart programming language and the Flutter UI toolkit. It is designed to complement theoretical learning with intensive programming practice, enabling students to develop practical skills through assignments and small-scale mobile application projects.
courses.description: This course covers Flutter installation, Dart fundamentals (variables, functions), Flutter basics such as Scaffold, AppBar, ElevatedButton, Text, and AlertDialog, stateful widgets, TextField, DropDownButton, asynchronous programming, navigation, fetching data from web services, parsing JSON data, ListViews, tab bars, list views, Firebase (Part 1-4), midterm, 2D animations and gestures (Part 1-2), and review of all topics.
learning_outcomes: Create beautiful, fast, and native-quality applications with Flutter.
Develop iOS and Android applications from a single codebase.
Understand all fundamental concepts of Flutter development.
Create applications that can access online and local data sources.

courses.name: Digital Signal Processing
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamentals of digital signal processing in the final year of engineering education. Students learn the basic concepts of discrete-time signal processing, transformation techniques, analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog conversion, and digital filter design, with practical applications to real systems supported by Matlab simulations.
courses.description: Introduction to discrete-time signals and systems, discrete-time LTI systems, Z-transform, sampling of continuous-time signals, multirate signal processing and an introduction to discrete-time random processes, analysis of LTI systems in different transform domains, block diagrams and IIR systems, FIR systems and the effect of digital sampling on systems, digital filter design techniques, discrete-time Fourier transform, and discrete-time random processes and systems.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamentals of linear time-invariant (LTI) systems and use transformation approaches in the analysis of LTI systems.
Sample low- and high-frequency signals.
Design IIR and FIR filters as LTI systems.
Effectively use digital frequency transformation techniques such as DFT and FFT.
Analyze discrete random systems in time and frequency domains.

courses.name: Embedded Systems
courses.purpose: To gain proficiency in the hardware description language (Verilog HDL) for the design of digital systems, including specification, simulation, and synthesis, and to apply them to FPGAs.
courses.description: This course covers digital systems review, FPGA systems, digital system modeling using Verilog, Verilog modeling styles: structural, dataflow, and behavioral, design verification, combinational circuit design using Verilog, sequential circuit design using Verilog, finite state machine design using Verilog, CPU design, synthesis, implementation of Verilog design on FPGA, and design optimization.
learning_outcomes: Model digital blocks structurally, with dataflow, and behaviorally using Verilog HDL
Model, simulate, and test combinational circuits in Verilog
Model, simulate, and test sequential circuits in Verilog
Digital system synthesis for FPGA
Digital system optimization for FPGA

courses.name: Introduction to Natural Language Processing
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a fundamental understanding of natural language processing (NLP), including the standard frameworks, algorithms, and techniques used to solve various NLP problems. It focuses especially on recent deep learning approaches and prepares students to understand and research state-of-the-art methods in NLP.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to natural language processing, linguistic fundamentals, regular expressions, text normalization, edit distance, n-gram models, machine learning fundamentals, text classification, Naive Bayes and logistic regression, vector semantics and dense word embeddings, neural networks and neural language models, sequence labeling for part-of-speech tagging and named entities, RNNs and LSTMs, transformers and pretrained language models, fine-tuning and masked language models, machine translation, question answering and information retrieval, chatbots and dialogue systems, automatic speech recognition and text-to-speech, context-free grammars, constituency parsing, dependency parsing, logical representations of sentence meaning, and topic review and project presentations.
learning_outcomes: Break down a real-world problem in NLP into subproblems, use available natural language processing tools to perform basic NLP, and identify potential solutions.
Learn the main uses of machine learning techniques and deep learning models in NLP.

Explain state-of-the-art methods for solving NLP subproblems such as text representation, representation learning techniques, text mining, language modeling, and similarity detection, and gain an understanding of methods and metrics for various natural language processing tasks and applications.
Automatically extract information from text using NLP concepts and methods including stemming, n-grams, POS tagging, and parsing.
Understand the terminology of a wide range of concepts and tasks in NLP.

courses.name: Advanced Software Techniques
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students learn, understand, and analyze modern software development methods, principles, and applications. It also covers requirements analysis and specification, the design, implementation, verification, and maintenance of large software systems, as well as advanced software development techniques and large project management approaches.
courses.description: Introduction to software methodologies and engineering, UML modeling, software construction, software process and plan-driven methods, agile methods, requirements engineering, elicitation and specification, software architecture, design patterns and architectural patterns, MVC and web application architectures, REST and web services, software modularity and system decomposition, software usability, personas and wireframes, software verification and validation, software test strategies, continuous integration, software security and maintenance.
learning_outcomes: Learn how to plan, design, and build a complex software system involving various aspects of modern software engineering.
Gain competence in using modern tools and overcoming challenging technical areas.
Gain experience working on a large software project as part of a team collaborating with other teams and customer representatives.

courses.name: Introduction to Medical Data Analysis
courses.description: This course covers topics related to medical data analysis.

courses.name: EMBEDDED ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE AND COMPUTER VISION
courses.purpose: To develop artificial intelligence and image processing applications on edge devices such as Nvidia Jetson.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to the Linux operating system, Nvidia Jetson Nano installation, face detection applications, CSI camera installation and usage, leveraging GPU functions in OpenCV, optical flow and object tracking applications, OpenCV DNN module usage, model optimization and usage with TensorRT, Mediapipe usage, Tesseract usage, Nvidia Jetson GPIO usage, semester project work (I), semester project work (II), and project presentation.
learning_outcomes: Develops artificial intelligence and image processing applications on resource-constrained platforms
Uses the Nvidia Jetson platform

courses.name: Introduction to Design Patterns
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the fundamental principles of software design patterns. Students will gain the ability to identify, apply, and use common design patterns to improve software architecture, increase code reuse, and ensure maintainability. The course provides hands-on experience in developing scalable and efficient software solutions through creational, structural, and behavioral design patterns.
courses.description: This course covers an introduction to software design patterns, object-oriented design principles, classification of design patterns, Singleton and Factory patterns, Abstract Factory and Builder patterns, Prototype pattern, Adapter and Bridge patterns, Composite and Decorator patterns, Facade and Proxy patterns, Strategy and Observer patterns, Command and State patterns, Template Method and Iterator patterns, Chain of Responsibility and Mediator patterns, and Memento and Visitor patterns.
learning_outcomes: Understand software design patterns
Identify appropriate design patterns
Apply design patterns
Increase software maintainability and reusability
Evaluate software design decisions
Develop practical problem-solving skills

courses.name: Introduction to Data Management and Processing
courses.purpose: This course introduces the basics of data management and processing. It covers important topics such as storage management, indexing, query processing and optimization, concurrency control, data recovery, data warehouses, data privacy, and security.
courses.description: This course covers storage management and indexing, queries and query processing, heuristic-based and cost-based query optimization, transaction management, concurrency control, recovery schemes, project presentations, data security, and privacy.
learning_outcomes: Understanding the basics of data storage and indexing
Understanding the basics of query processing and optimization
Understanding the basics of concurrency control
Understanding the basics of data recovery
Understanding of basics of data warehouse management
Understanding of basics of data privacy and security
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course material, overview of data management and processing

courses.description: This course covers topics related to large language models.

courses.name: Human-Computer Interaction
courses.purpose: This course covers the fundamental concepts, basic theories, and current research in human-computer interaction. It aims to introduce students to the core concepts of human-computer interaction, the theory and principles of computer interface design, the process of designing user interfaces, and the skills needed to design, conduct, and analyze user studies for computer software.
courses.description: This course covers interactive design, the interaction design process, conceptualizing interaction, cognitive aspects, interfaces, discovering requirements, data collection, data analysis, interpretation and presentation, midterm presentation, design, prototyping and construction, data at different scales, interaction design in practice, introduction to evaluation, evaluation studies from controlled to natural settings, evaluation through inspection, analytical and model-based methods, social interaction, emotional interaction, and final presentation.
learning_outcomes: Applies fundamental concepts in human-computer interaction
Designs and conducts user experiments for computer interfaces
Analyzes data collected from user experiments
Designs computer interfaces to meet desired needs within realistic constraints
Communicates effectively with stakeholders
